verses.halfverse: from age to age. Amen. Amen.

verses.verse: As the deer longs for the water-brooks, *
verses.halfverse: so longs my soul for you, O God.

verses.verse: My soul is athirst for God, athirst for the living God; *
verses.halfverse: when shall I come to appear before the presence of God?

verses.verse: My tears have been my food day and night, *
verses.halfverse: while all day long they say to me, "Where now is your God?"

verses.verse: I pour out my soul when I think on these things; *
verses.halfverse: how I went with the multitude and led them into the house of God,

verses.verse: With the voice of praise and thanksgiving, *
verses.halfverse: among those who keep holy-day.

verses.verse: Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? *
verses.halfverse: and why are you so disquieted within me?

verses.verse: Put your trust in God; *
verses.halfverse: for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.

verses.verse: My soul is heavy within me; *
verses.halfverse: therefore I will remember you from the land of Jordan, and from the peak of Mizar among the heights of Hermon.

verses.verse: One deep calls to another in the noise of your cataracts; *
verses.halfverse: all your rapids and floods have gone over me.

verses.verse: The LORD grants his loving-kindness in the daytime; *
verses.halfverse: in the night season his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.

verses.verse: I will say to the God of my strength, "Why have you forgotten me? *
verses.halfverse: and why do I go so heavily while the enemy oppresses me?"

verses.verse: While my bones are being broken, *
verses.halfverse: my enemies mock me to my face;

verses.verse: All day long they mock me *
verses.halfverse: and say to me, "Where now is your God?"

verses.verse: Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? *
verses.halfverse: and why are you so disquieted within me?

verses.verse: Put your trust in God; *
verses.halfverse: for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.

verses.verse: Give judgment for me, O God, and defend my cause against an ungodly people; *
verses.halfverse: deliver me from the deceitful and the wicked.

verses.verse: For you are the God of my strength; why have you put me from you? *
verses.halfverse: and why do I go so heavily while the enemy oppresses me?

verses.verse: Send out your light and your truth, that they may lead me, *
verses.halfverse: and bring me to your holy hill and to your dwelling;

verses.verse: That I may go to the altar of God, to the God of my joy and gladness; *
verses.halfverse: and on the harp I will give thanks to you, O God my God.

verses.verse: Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? *
verses.halfverse: and why are you so disquieted within me?

verses.verse: Put your trust in God; *
verses.halfverse: for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.

verses.verse: We have heard with our ears, O God, our forefathers have told us, *
verses.halfverse: the deeds you did in their days, in the days of old.

verses.verse: How with your hand you drove the peoples out and planted our forefathers in the land; *
verses.halfverse: how you destroyed nations and made your people flourish.

verses.verse: For they did not take the land by their sword, nor did their arm win the victory for them; *
verses.halfverse: but your right hand, your arm, and the light of your countenance, because you favored them.

verses.verse: You are my King and my God; *
verses.halfverse: you command victories for Jacob.

verses.verse: Through you we pushed back our adversaries; *
verses.halfverse: through your Name we trampled on those who rose up against us.

verses.verse: For I do not rely on my bow, *
verses.halfverse: and my sword does not give me the victory.

verses.verse: Surely, you gave us victory over our adversaries *
verses.halfverse: and put those who hate us to shame.

verses.verse: Every day we gloried in God, *
verses.halfverse: and we will praise your Name for ever.

verses.verse: Nevertheless, you have rejected and humbled us *
verses.halfverse: and do not go forth with our armies.

verses.verse: You have made us fall back before our adversary, *
verses.halfverse: and our enemies have plundered us.

verses.verse: You have made us like sheep to be eaten *
verses.halfverse: and have scattered us among the nations.

verses.verse: You are selling your people for a trifle *
verses.halfverse: and are making no profit on the sale of them.

verses.verse: You have made us the scorn of our neighbors, *
verses.halfverse: a mockery and derision to those around us.

verses.verse: You have made us a byword among the nations, *
verses.halfverse: a laughing-stock among the peoples.

verses.verse: My humiliation is daily before me, *
verses.halfverse: and shame has covered my face;

verses.verse: Because of the taunts of the mockers and blasphemers, *
verses.halfverse: because of the enemy and avenger.

verses.verse: All this has come upon us; *
verses.halfverse: yet we have not forgotten you, nor have we betrayed your covenant.

verses.verse: Our heart never turned back, *
verses.halfverse: nor did our footsteps stray from your path;

verses.verse: Though you thrust us down into a place of misery, *
verses.halfverse: and covered us over with deep darkness.

verses.verse: If we have forgotten the Name of our God, *
verses.halfverse: or stretched out our hands to some strange god,

verses.verse: Will not God find it out? *
verses.halfverse: for he knows the secrets of the heart.

verses.verse: Indeed, for your sake we are killed all the day long; *
verses.halfverse: we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.

verses.verse: Awake, O Lord! why are you sleeping? *
verses.halfverse: Arise! do not reject us for ever.

verses.verse: Why have you hidden your face *
verses.halfverse: and forgotten our affliction and oppression?

verses.verse: We sink down into the dust; *
verses.halfverse: our body cleaves to the ground.

verses.verse: Rise up, and help us, *
verses.halfverse: and save us, for the sake of your steadfast love.

verses.verse: My heart is stirring with a noble song; let me recite what I have fashioned for the king; *
verses.halfverse: my tongue shall be the pen of a skilled writer.

verses.verse: You are the fairest of men; *
verses.halfverse: grace flows from your lips, because God has blessed you for ever.

verses.verse: Strap your sword upon your thigh, O mighty warrior, *
verses.halfverse: in your pride and in your majesty.

verses.verse: Ride out and conquer in the cause of truth *
verses.halfverse: and for the sake of justice.

verses.verse: Your right hand will show you marvelous things; *
verses.halfverse: your arrows are very sharp, O mighty warrior.

verses.verse: The peoples are falling at your feet, *
verses.halfverse: and the king's enemies are losing heart.

verses.verse: Your throne, O God, endures for ever and ever, *
verses.halfverse: a scepter of righteousness is the scepter of your kingdom; you love righteousness and hate iniquity.

verses.verse: Therefore God, your God, has anointed you *
verses.halfverse: with the oil of gladness above your fellows.

verses.verse: All your garments are fragrant with myrrh, aloes, and cassia, *
verses.halfverse: and the music of strings from ivory palaces makes you glad.

verses.verse: Kings' daughters stand among the ladies of the court; *
verses.halfverse: on your right hand is the queen, adorned with the gold of Ophir.

verses.verse: "Hear, O daughter; consider and listen closely; *
verses.halfverse: forget your people and your father's house.

verses.verse: The king will have pleasure in your beauty; *
verses.halfverse: he is your master; therefore do him honor.

verses.verse: The people of Tyre are here with a gift; *
verses.halfverse: the rich among the people seek your favor."

verses.verse: All glorious is the princess as she enters; *
verses.halfverse: her gown is cloth-of-gold.

verses.verse: In embroidered apparel she is brought to the king; *
verses.halfverse: after her the bridesmaids follow in procession.

verses.verse: With joy and gladness they are brought, *
verses.halfverse: and enter into the palace of the king.

verses.verse: "In place of fathers, O king, you shall have sons; *
verses.halfverse: you shall make them princes over all the earth.

verses.verse: I will make your name to be remembered from one generation to another; *
verses.halfverse: therefore nations will praise you for ever and ever."

verses.verse: God is our refuge and strength, *
verses.halfverse: a very present help in trouble.

verses.verse: Therefore we will not fear, though the earth be moved, *
verses.halfverse: and though the mountains be toppled into the depths of the sea;

verses.verse: Though its waters rage and foam, *
verses.halfverse: and though the mountains tremble at its tumult.

verses.verse: The LORD of hosts is with us; *
verses.halfverse: the God of Jacob is our stronghold.

verses.verse: There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God, *
verses.halfverse: the holy habitation of the Most High.

verses.verse: God is in the midst of her; she shall not be overthrown; *
verses.halfverse: God shall help her at the break of day.

verses.verse: The nations make much ado, and the kingdoms are shaken; *
verses.halfverse: God has spoken, and the earth shall melt away.

verses.verse: The LORD of hosts is with us; *
verses.halfverse: the God of Jacob is our stronghold.

verses.verse: Come now and look upon the works of the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: what awesome things he has done on earth.

verses.verse: It is he who makes war to cease in all the world; *
verses.halfverse: he breaks the bow, and shatters the spear, and burns the shields with fire.

verses.verse: "Be still, then, and know that I am God; *
verses.halfverse: I will be exalted among the nations; I will be exalted in the earth."

verses.verse: The LORD of hosts is with us; *
verses.halfverse: the God of Jacob is our stronghold.

verses.verse: Clap your hands, all you peoples; *
verses.halfverse: shout to God with a cry of joy.

verses.verse: For the LORD Most High is to be feared; *
verses.halfverse: he is the great King over all the earth.

verses.verse: He subdues the peoples under us, *
verses.halfverse: and the nations under our feet.

verses.verse: He chooses our inheritance for us, *
verses.halfverse: the pride of Jacob whom he loves.

verses.verse: God has gone up with a shout, *
verses.halfverse: the LORD with the sound of the ram's-horn.

verses.verse: Sing praises to God, sing praises; *
verses.halfverse: sing praises to our King, sing praises.

verses.verse: For God is King of all the earth; *
verses.halfverse: sing praises with all your skill.

verses.verse: God reigns over the nations; *
verses.halfverse: God sits upon his holy throne.

verses.verse: The nobles of the peoples have gathered together *
verses.halfverse: with the people of the God of Abraham.

verses.verse: The rulers of the earth belong to God, *
verses.halfverse: and he is highly exalted.

verses.verse: Great is the LORD, and highly to be praised; *
verses.halfverse: in the city of our God is his holy hill.

verses.verse: Beautiful and lofty, the joy of all the earth, is the hill of Zion, *
verses.halfverse: the very center of the world and the city of the great King.

verses.verse: God is in her citadels; *
verses.halfverse: he is known to be her sure refuge.

verses.verse: Behold, the kings of the earth assembled *
verses.halfverse: and marched forward together.

verses.verse: They looked and were astounded; *
verses.halfverse: they retreated and fled in terror.

verses.verse: Trembling seized them there; *
verses.halfverse: they writhed like a woman in childbirth, like ships of the sea when the east wind shatters them.

verses.verse: As we have heard, so have we seen, in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God; *
verses.halfverse: God has established her for ever.

verses.verse: We have waited in silence on your loving-kindness, O God, *
verses.halfverse: in the midst of your temple.

verses.verse: Your praise, like your Name, O God, reaches to the world's end; *
verses.halfverse: your right hand is full of justice.

verses.verse: Let Mount Zion be glad and the cities of Judah rejoice, *
verses.halfverse: because of your judgments.

verses.verse: Make the circuit of Zion; walk round about her; *
verses.halfverse: count the number of her towers.

verses.verse: Consider well her bulwarks; examine her strongholds; *
verses.halfverse: that you may tell those who come after.

verses.verse: This God is our God for ever and ever; *
verses.halfverse: he shall be our guide for evermore.

verses.verse: Hear this, all you peoples; hearken, all you who dwell in the world, *
verses.halfverse: you of high degree and low, rich and poor together.

verses.verse: My mouth shall speak of wisdom, *
verses.halfverse: and my heart shall meditate on understanding.

verses.verse: I will incline my ear to a proverb *
verses.halfverse: and set forth my riddle upon the harp.

verses.verse: Why should I be afraid in evil days, *
verses.halfverse: when the wickedness of those at my heels surrounds me,

verses.verse: The wickedness of those who put their trust in their goods, *
verses.halfverse: and boast of their great riches?

verses.verse: We can never ransom ourselves, *
verses.halfverse: or deliver to God the price of our life;

verses.verse: For the ransom of our life is so great, *
verses.halfverse: that we should never have enough to pay it,

verses.verse: In order to live for ever and ever, *
verses.halfverse: and never see the grave.

verses.verse: For we see that the wise die also; like the dull and stupid they perish *
verses.halfverse: and leave their wealth to those who come after them.

verses.verse: Their graves shall be their homes for ever, their dwelling places from generation to generation, *
verses.halfverse: though they call the lands after their own names.

verses.verse: Even though honored, they cannot live for ever; *
verses.halfverse: they are like the beasts that perish.

verses.verse: Such is the way of those who foolishly trust in themselves, *
verses.halfverse: and the end of those who delight in their own words.

verses.verse: Like a flock of sheep they are destined to die; Death is their shepherd; *
verses.halfverse: they go down straightway to the grave.

verses.verse: Their form shall waste away, *
verses.halfverse: and the land of the dead shall be their home.

verses.verse: But God will ransom my life; *
verses.halfverse: he will snatch me from the grasp of death.

verses.verse: Do not be envious when some become rich, *
verses.halfverse: or when the grandeur of their house increases;

verses.verse: For they will carry nothing away at their death, *
verses.halfverse: nor will their grandeur follow them.

verses.verse: Though they thought highly of themselves while they lived, *
verses.halfverse: and were praised for their success,

verses.verse: They shall join the company of their forebears, *
verses.halfverse: who will never see the light again.

verses.verse: Those who are honored, but have no understanding, *
verses.halfverse: are like the beasts that perish.

verses.verse: The LORD, the God of gods, has spoken; *
verses.halfverse: he has called the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.

verses.verse: Out of Zion, perfect in its beauty, *
verses.halfverse: God reveals himself in glory.

verses.verse: Our God will come and will not keep silence; *
verses.halfverse: before him there is a consuming flame, and round about him a raging storm.

verses.verse: He calls the heavens and the earth from above *
verses.halfverse: to witness the judgment of his people.

verses.verse: "Gather before me my loyal followers, *
verses.halfverse: those who have made a covenant with me and sealed it with sacrifice."

verses.verse: Let the heavens declare the rightness of his cause; *
verses.halfverse: for God himself is judge.

verses.verse: Hear, O my people, and I will speak: "O Israel, I will bear witness against you; *
verses.halfverse: for I am God, your God.

verses.verse: I do not accuse you because of your sacrifices; *
verses.halfverse: your offerings are always before me.

verses.verse: I will take no bull-calf from your stalls, *
verses.halfverse: nor he-goats out of your pens;

verses.verse: For all the beasts of the forest are mine, *
verses.halfverse: the herds in their thousands upon the hills.

verses.verse: I know every bird in the sky, *
verses.halfverse: and the creatures of the fields are in my sight.

verses.verse: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, *
verses.halfverse: for the whole world is mine and all that is in it.

verses.verse: Do you think I eat the flesh of bulls, *
verses.halfverse: or drink the blood of goats?

verses.verse: Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving *
verses.halfverse: and make good your vows to the Most High.

verses.verse: Call upon me in the day of trouble; *
verses.halfverse: I will deliver you, and you shall honor me."

verses.verse: But to the wicked God says: *
verses.halfverse: Why do you recite my statutes, and take my covenant upon your lips;

verses.verse: Since you refuse discipline, *
verses.halfverse: and toss my words behind your back?

verses.verse: When you see a thief, you make him your friend, *
verses.halfverse: and you cast in your lot with adulterers.

verses.verse: You have loosed your lips for evil, *
verses.halfverse: and harnessed your tongue to a lie.

verses.verse: You are always speaking evil of your brother *
verses.halfverse: and slandering your own mother's son.

verses.verse: These things you have done, and I kept still, *
verses.halfverse: and you thought that I am like you."

verses.verse: "I have made my accusation; *
verses.halfverse: I have put my case in order before your eyes.

verses.verse: Consider this well, you who forget God, *
verses.halfverse: lest I rend you and there be none to deliver you.

verses.verse: Whoever offers me the sacrifice of thanksgiving honors me; *
verses.halfverse: but to those who keep in my way will I show the salvation of God."

verses.verse: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your loving-kindness; *
verses.halfverse: in your great compassion blot out my offenses.

verses.verse: Wash me through and through from my wickedness *
verses.halfverse: and cleanse me from my sin.

verses.verse: For I know my transgressions, *
verses.halfverse: and my sin is ever before me.

verses.verse: Against you only have I sinned *
verses.halfverse: and done what is evil in your sight.

verses.verse: And so you are justified when you speak *
verses.halfverse: and upright in your judgment.

verses.verse: Indeed, I have been wicked from my birth, *
verses.halfverse: a sinner from my mother's womb.

verses.verse: For behold, you look for truth deep within me, *
verses.halfverse: and will make me understand wisdom secretly.

verses.verse: Purge me from my sin, and I shall be pure; *
verses.halfverse: wash me, and I shall be clean indeed.

verses.verse: Make me hear of joy and gladness, *
verses.halfverse: that the body you have broken may rejoice.

verses.verse: Hide your face from my sins *
verses.halfverse: and blot out all my iniquities.

verses.verse: Create in me a clean heart, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and renew a right spirit within me.

verses.verse: Cast me not away from your presence *
verses.halfverse: and take not your holy Spirit from me.

verses.verse: Give me the joy of your saving help again *
verses.halfverse: and sustain me with your bountiful Spirit.

verses.verse: I shall teach your ways to the wicked, *
verses.halfverse: and sinners shall return to you.

verses.verse: Deliver me from death, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and my tongue shall sing of your righteousness, O God of my salvation.

verses.verse: Open my lips, O Lord, *
verses.halfverse: and my mouth shall proclaim your praise.

verses.verse: Had you desired it, I would have offered sacrifice, *
verses.halfverse: but you take no delight in burnt-offerings.

verses.verse: The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit; *
verses.halfverse: a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.

verses.verse: Be favorable and gracious to Zion, *
verses.halfverse: and rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.

verses.verse: Then you will be pleased with the appointed sacrifices, with burnt-offerings and oblations; *
verses.halfverse: then shall they offer young bullocks upon your altar.

verses.verse: You tyrant, why do you boast of wickedness *
verses.halfverse: against the godly all day long?

verses.verse: You plot ruin; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, *
verses.halfverse: O worker of deception.

verses.verse: You love evil more than good *
verses.halfverse: and lying more than speaking the truth.

verses.verse: You love all words that hurt, *
verses.halfverse: O you deceitful tongue.

verses.verse: Oh, that God would demolish you utterly, *
verses.halfverse: topple you, and snatch you from your dwelling, and root you out of the land of the living!

verses.verse: The righteous shall see and tremble, *
verses.halfverse: and they shall laugh at him, saying,

verses.verse: "This is the one who did not take God for a refuge, *
verses.halfverse: but trusted in great wealth and relied upon wickedness."

verses.verse: But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; *
verses.halfverse: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.

verses.verse: I will give you thanks for what you have done *
verses.halfverse: and declare the goodness of your Name in the presence of the godly.

verses.verse: The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." *
verses.halfverse: All are corrupt and commit abominable acts; there is none who does any good.

verses.verse: God looks down from heaven upon us all, *
verses.halfverse: to see if there is any who is wise, if there is one who seeks after God.

verses.verse: Every one has proved faithless; all alike have turned bad; *
verses.halfverse: there is none who does good; no, not one.

verses.verse: Have they no knowledge, those evildoers *
verses.halfverse: who eat up my people like bread and do not call upon God?

verses.verse: See how greatly they tremble, such trembling as never was; *
verses.halfverse: for God has scattered the bones of the enemy; they are put to shame, because God has rejected them.

verses.verse: Oh, that Israel's deliverance would come out of Zion! *
verses.halfverse: when God restores the fortunes of his people Jacob will rejoice and Israel be glad.

verses.verse: Save me, O God, by your Name; *
verses.halfverse: in your might, defend my cause.

verses.verse: Hear my prayer, O God; *
verses.halfverse: give ear to the words of my mouth.

verses.verse: For the arrogant have risen up against me, and the ruthless have sought my life, *
verses.halfverse: those who have no regard for God.

verses.verse: Behold, God is my helper; *
verses.halfverse: it is the Lord who sustains my life.

verses.verse: Render evil to those who spy on me; *
verses.halfverse: in your faithfulness, destroy them.

verses.verse: I will offer you a freewill sacrifice *
verses.halfverse: and praise your Name, O LORD, for it is good.

verses.verse: For you have rescued me from every trouble, *
verses.halfverse: and my eye has seen the ruin of my foes.

verses.verse: Hear my prayer, O God; *
verses.halfverse: do not hide yourself from my petition.

verses.verse: Listen to me and answer me; *
verses.halfverse: I have no peace, because of my cares.

verses.verse: I am shaken by the noise of the enemy *
verses.halfverse: and by the pressure of the wicked;

verses.verse: For they have cast an evil spell upon me *
verses.halfverse: and are set against me in fury.

verses.verse: My heart quakes within me, *
verses.halfverse: and the terrors of death have fallen upon me.

verses.verse: Fear and trembling have come over me, *
verses.halfverse: and horror overwhelms me.

verses.verse: And I said, "Oh, that I had wings like a dove! *
verses.halfverse: I would fly away and be at rest.

verses.verse: I would flee to a far-off place *
verses.halfverse: and make my lodging in the wilderness.

verses.verse: I would hasten to escape *
verses.halfverse: from the stormy wind and tempest."

verses.verse: Swallow them up, O Lord; confound their speech; *
verses.halfverse: for I have seen violence and strife in the city.

verses.verse: Day and night the watchmen make their rounds upon her walls, *
verses.halfverse: but trouble and misery are in the midst of her.

verses.verse: There is corruption at her heart; *
verses.halfverse: her streets are never free of oppression and deceit.

verses.verse: For had it been an adversary who taunted me, then I could have borne it; *
verses.halfverse: or had it been an enemy who vaunted himself against me, then I could have hidden from him.

verses.verse: But it was you, a man after my own heart, *
verses.halfverse: my companion, my own familiar friend.

verses.verse: We took sweet counsel together, *
verses.halfverse: and walked with the throng in the house of God.

verses.verse: Let death come upon them suddenly; let them go down alive to the grave; *
verses.halfverse: for wickedness is in their dwellings, in their very midst.

verses.verse: But I will call upon God, *
verses.halfverse: and the LORD will deliver me.

verses.verse: In the evening, in the morning, and at noonday, I will complain and lament, *
verses.halfverse: and he will hear my voice.

verses.verse: He will bring me safely back from the battle waged against me; *
verses.halfverse: for there are many who fight me.

verses.verse: God, who is enthroned of old, will hear me and bring them down; *
verses.halfverse: they never change; they do not fear God.

verses.verse: My companion stretched forth his hand against his comrade; *
verses.halfverse: he has broken his covenant.

verses.verse: His speech is softer than butter, *
verses.halfverse: but war is in his heart.

verses.verse: His words are smoother than oil, *
verses.halfverse: but they are drawn swords.

verses.verse: Cast your burden upon the LORD, and he will sustain you; *
verses.halfverse: he will never let the righteous stumble.

verses.verse: For you will bring the bloodthirsty and deceitful *
verses.halfverse: down to the pit of destruction, O God.

verses.verse: They shall not live out half their days, *
verses.halfverse: but I will put my trust in you.

verses.verse: Have mercy on me, O God, for my enemies are hounding me; *
verses.halfverse: all day long they assault and oppress me.

verses.verse: They hound me all the day long; *
verses.halfverse: truly there are many who fight against me, O Most High.

verses.verse: Whenever I am afraid, *
verses.halfverse: I will put my trust in you.

verses.verse: In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, *
verses.halfverse: for what can flesh do to me?

verses.verse: All day long they damage my cause; *
verses.halfverse: their only thought is to do me evil.

verses.verse: They band together; they lie in wait; *
verses.halfverse: they spy upon my footsteps; because they seek my life.

verses.verse: Shall they escape despite their wickedness? *
verses.halfverse: O God, in your anger, cast down the peoples.

verses.verse: You have noted my lamentation; put my tears into your bottle; *
verses.halfverse: are they not recorded in your book?

verses.verse: Whenever I call upon you, my enemies will be put to flight; *
verses.halfverse: this I know, for God is on my side.

verses.verse: In God the LORD, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, *
verses.halfverse: for what can mortals do to me?

verses.verse: I am bound by the vow I made to you, O God; *
verses.halfverse: I will present to you thank-offerings;

verses.verse: For you have rescued my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, *
verses.halfverse: that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

verses.verse: Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful, for I have taken refuge in you; *
verses.halfverse: in the shadow of your wings will I take refuge until this time of trouble has gone by.

verses.verse: I will call upon the Most High God, *
verses.halfverse: the God who maintains my cause.

verses.verse: He will send from heaven and save me; he will confound those who trample upon me; *
verses.halfverse: God will send forth his love and his faithfulness.

verses.verse: I lie in the midst of lions that devour the people; *
verses.halfverse: their teeth are spears and arrows, their tongue a sharp sword.

verses.verse: They have laid a net for my feet, and I am bowed low; *
verses.halfverse: they have dug a pit before me, but have fallen into it themselves.

verses.verse: Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and your glory over all the earth.

verses.verse: My heart is firmly fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; *
verses.halfverse: I will sing and make melody.

verses.verse: Wake up, my spirit; awake, lute and harp; *
verses.halfverse: I myself will waken the dawn.

verses.verse: I will confess you among the peoples, O LORD; *
verses.halfverse: I will sing praise to you among the nations.

verses.verse: For your loving-kindness is greater than the heavens, *
verses.halfverse: and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.

verses.verse: Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and your glory over all the earth.

verses.verse: Do you indeed decree righteousness, you rulers? *
verses.halfverse: do you judge the peoples with equity?

verses.verse: No; you devise evil in your hearts, *
verses.halfverse: and your hands deal out violence in the land.

verses.verse: The wicked are perverse from the womb; *
verses.halfverse: liars go astray from their birth.

verses.verse: They are as venomous as a serpent, *
verses.halfverse: they are like the deaf adder which stops its ears,

verses.verse: Which does not heed the voice of the charmer, *
verses.halfverse: no matter how skillful his charming.

verses.verse: O God, break their teeth in their mouths; *
verses.halfverse: pull the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.

verses.verse: Let them vanish like water that runs off; *
verses.halfverse: let them wither like trodden grass.

verses.verse: Let them be like the snail that melts away, *
verses.halfverse: like a stillborn child that never sees the sun.

verses.verse: Before they bear fruit, let them be cut down like a brier; *
verses.halfverse: like thorns and thistles let them be swept away.

verses.verse: The righteous will be glad when they see the vengeance; *
verses.halfverse: they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.

verses.verse: And they will say, "Surely, there is a reward for the righteous; *
verses.halfverse: surely, there is a God who rules in the earth."

verses.verse: Rescue me from my enemies, O God; *
verses.halfverse: protect me from those who rise up against me.

verses.verse: Rescue me from evildoers *
verses.halfverse: and save me from those who thirst for my blood.

verses.verse: See how they lie in wait for my life, how the mighty gather together against me; *
verses.halfverse: not for any offense or fault of mine, O LORD.

verses.verse: Not because of any guilt of mine *
verses.halfverse: they run and prepare themselves for battle.

verses.verse: Rouse yourself, come to my side, and see; *
verses.halfverse: for you, LORD God of hosts, are Israel's God.

verses.verse: Awake, and punish all the ungodly; *
verses.halfverse: show no mercy to those who are faithless and evil.

verses.verse: They go to and fro in the evening; *
verses.halfverse: they snarl like dogs and run about the city.

verses.verse: Behold, they boast with their mouths, and taunts are on their lips; *
verses.halfverse: "For who," they say, "will hear us?"

verses.verse: But you, O LORD, you laugh at them; *
verses.halfverse: you laugh all the ungodly to scorn.

verses.verse: My eyes are fixed on you, O my Strength; *
verses.halfverse: for you, O God, are my stronghold.

verses.verse: My merciful God comes to meet me; *
verses.halfverse: God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.

verses.verse: Slay them, O God, lest my people forget; *
verses.halfverse: send them reeling by your might and put them down, O Lord our shield.

verses.verse: For the sins of their mouths, for the words of their lips, for the cursing and lies that they utter, *
verses.halfverse: let them be caught in their pride.

verses.verse: Make an end of them in your wrath; *
verses.halfverse: make an end of them, and they shall be no more.

verses.verse: Let everyone know that God rules in Jacob, *
verses.halfverse: and to the ends of the earth.

verses.verse: They go to and fro in the evening; *
verses.halfverse: they snarl like dogs and run about the city.

verses.verse: They forage for food, *
verses.halfverse: and if they are not filled, they howl.

verses.verse: For my part, I will sing of your strength; *
verses.halfverse: I will celebrate your love in the morning;

verses.verse: For you have become my stronghold, *
verses.halfverse: a refuge in the day of my trouble.

verses.verse: To you, O my Strength, will I sing; *
verses.halfverse: for you, O God, are my stronghold and my merciful God.

verses.verse: O God, you have cast us off and broken us; *
verses.halfverse: you have been angry; oh, take us back to you again.

verses.verse: You have shaken the earth and split it open; *
verses.halfverse: repair the cracks in it, for it totters.

verses.verse: You have made your people know hardship; *
verses.halfverse: you have given us wine that makes us stagger.

verses.verse: You have set up a banner for those who fear you, *
verses.halfverse: to be a refuge from the power of the bow.

verses.verse: Save us by your right hand and answer us, *
verses.halfverse: that those who are dear to you may be delivered.

verses.verse: God spoke from his holy place and said: *
verses.halfverse: "I will exult and parcel out Shechem; I will divide the valley of Succoth.

verses.verse: Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; *
verses.halfverse: Ephraim is my helmet and Judah my scepter.

verses.verse: Moab is my wash-basin, on Edom I throw down my sandal to claim it, *
verses.halfverse: and over Philistia will I shout in triumph."

verses.verse: Who will lead me into the strong city? *
verses.halfverse: who will bring me to Edom?

verses.verse: Have you not cast us off, O God? *
verses.halfverse: you no longer go out, O God, with our armies.

verses.verse: Grant us your help against the enemy, *
verses.halfverse: for vain is the help of man.

verses.verse: With God we will do valiant deeds, *
verses.halfverse: and he shall tread our enemies under foot.

verses.verse: Hear my cry, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and listen to my prayer.

verses.verse: I call upon you from the ends of the earth with heaviness in my heart; *
verses.halfverse: set me upon the rock that is higher than I.

verses.verse: For you have been my refuge, *
verses.halfverse: a strong tower against the enemy.

verses.verse: I will dwell in your house for ever; *
verses.halfverse: I will take refuge under the cover of your wings.

verses.verse: For you, O God, have heard my vows; *
verses.halfverse: you have granted me the heritage of those who fear your Name.

verses.verse: Add length of days to the king's life; *
verses.halfverse: let his years extend over many generations.

verses.verse: Let him sit enthroned before God for ever; *
verses.halfverse: bid love and faithfulness watch over him.

verses.verse: So will I always sing the praise of your Name, *
verses.halfverse: and day by day I will fulfill my vows.

verses.verse: For God alone my soul in silence waits; *
verses.halfverse: from him comes my salvation.

verses.verse: He alone is my rock and my salvation, *
verses.halfverse: my stronghold, so that I shall not be greatly shaken.

verses.verse: How long will you assail me to crush me, all of you together, *
verses.halfverse: as if you were a leaning fence, a toppling wall?

verses.verse: They seek only to bring me down from my place of honor; *
verses.halfverse: lies are their chief delight.

verses.verse: They bless with their lips, *
verses.halfverse: but in their hearts they curse.

verses.verse: For God alone my soul in silence waits; *
verses.halfverse: truly, my hope is in him.

verses.verse: He alone is my rock and my salvation, *
verses.halfverse: my stronghold, so that I shall not be shaken.

verses.verse: In God is my safety and my honor; *
verses.halfverse: God is my strong rock and my refuge.

verses.verse: Put your trust in him always, O people, *
verses.halfverse: pour out your hearts before him, for God is our refuge.

verses.verse: Those of high degree are but a fleeting breath, *
verses.halfverse: even those of low estate cannot be trusted.

verses.verse: On the scales they are lighter than a breath, *
verses.halfverse: all of them together.

verses.verse: Put no trust in extortion; in robbery take no empty pride; *
verses.halfverse: though wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.

verses.verse: God has spoken once, twice have I heard it, *
verses.halfverse: that power belongs to God.

verses.verse: Steadfast love is yours, O Lord, *
verses.halfverse: for you repay everyone according to his deeds.

verses.verse: O God, you are my God; eagerly I seek you; *
verses.halfverse: my soul thirsts for you, my flesh faints for you, as in a barren and dry land where there is no water.

verses.verse: Therefore I have gazed upon you in your holy place, *
verses.halfverse: that I might behold your power and your glory.

verses.verse: For your loving-kindness is better than life itself; *
verses.halfverse: my lips shall give you praise.

verses.verse: So will I bless you as long as I live *
verses.halfverse: and lift up my hands in your Name.

verses.verse: My soul is content, as with marrow and fatness, *
verses.halfverse: and my mouth praises you with joyful lips,

verses.verse: When I remember you upon my bed, *
verses.halfverse: and meditate on you in the night watches.

verses.verse: For you have been my helper, *
verses.halfverse: and under the shadow of your wings I will rejoice.

verses.verse: My soul clings to you; *
verses.halfverse: your right hand holds me fast.

verses.verse: May those who seek my life to destroy it *
verses.halfverse: go down into the depths of the earth;

verses.verse: Let them fall upon the edge of the sword, *
verses.halfverse: and let them be food for jackals.

verses.verse: But the king will rejoice in God; all those who swear by him will be glad; *
verses.halfverse: for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be stopped.

verses.verse: Hear my voice, O God, when I complain; *
verses.halfverse: protect my life from fear of the enemy.

verses.verse: Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, *
verses.halfverse: from the mob of evildoers.

verses.verse: They sharpen their tongue like a sword, *
verses.halfverse: and aim their bitter words like arrows,

verses.verse: That they may shoot down the blameless from ambush; *
verses.halfverse: they shoot without warning and are not afraid.

verses.verse: They hold fast to their evil course; *
verses.halfverse: they plan how they may hide their snares.

verses.verse: They say, "Who will see us? who will find out our crimes? *
verses.halfverse: we have thought out a perfect plot."

verses.verse: The human mind and heart are a mystery; *
verses.halfverse: but God will loose an arrow at them, and suddenly they will be wounded.

verses.verse: He will make them trip over their tongues, *
verses.halfverse: and all who see them will shake their heads.

verses.verse: Everyone will stand in awe and declare God's deeds; *
verses.halfverse: they will recognize his works.

verses.verse: The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and put their trust in him, *
verses.halfverse: and all who are true of heart will glory.

verses.verse: You are to be praised, O God, in Zion; *
verses.halfverse: to you shall vows be performed in Jerusalem.

verses.verse: To you that hear prayer shall all flesh come, *
verses.halfverse: because of their transgressions.

verses.verse: Our sins are stronger than we are, *
verses.halfverse: but you will blot them out.

verses.verse: Happy are they whom you choose and draw to your courts to dwell there! *
verses.halfverse: they will be satisfied by the beauty of your house, by the holiness of your temple.

verses.verse: Awesome things will you show us in your righteousness, O God of our salvation, *
verses.halfverse: O Hope of all the ends of the earth and of the seas that are far away.

verses.verse: You make fast the mountains by your power; *
verses.halfverse: they are girded about with might.

verses.verse: You still the roaring of the seas, *
verses.halfverse: the roaring of their waves, and the clamor of the peoples.

verses.verse: Those who dwell at the ends of the earth will tremble at your marvelous signs; *
verses.halfverse: you make the dawn and the dusk to sing for joy.

verses.verse: You visit the earth and water it abundantly; you make it very plenteous; *
verses.halfverse: the river of God is full of water.

verses.verse: You prepare the grain, *
verses.halfverse: for so you provide for the earth.

verses.verse: You drench the furrows and smooth out the ridges; *
verses.halfverse: with heavy rain you soften the ground and bless its increase.

verses.verse: You crown the year with your goodness, *
verses.halfverse: and your paths overflow with plenty.

verses.verse: May the fields of the wilderness be rich for grazing, *
verses.halfverse: and the hills be clothed with joy.

verses.verse: May the meadows cover themselves with flocks, and the valleys cloak themselves with grain; *
verses.halfverse: let them shout for joy and sing.

verses.verse: Be joyful in God, all you lands; *
verses.halfverse: sing the glory of his Name; sing the glory of his praise.

verses.verse: Say to God, "How awesome are your deeds! *
verses.halfverse: because of your great strength your enemies cringe before you.

verses.verse: All the earth bows down before you, *
verses.halfverse: sings to you, sings out your Name."

verses.verse: Come now and see the works of God, *
verses.halfverse: how wonderful he is in his doing toward all people.

verses.verse: He turned the sea into dry land, so that they went through the water on foot, *
verses.halfverse: and there we rejoiced in him.

verses.verse: In his might he rules for ever; his eyes keep watch over the nations; *
verses.halfverse: let no rebel rise up against him.

verses.verse: Bless our God, you peoples; *
verses.halfverse: make the voice of his praise to be heard;

verses.verse: Who holds our souls in life, *
verses.halfverse: and will not allow our feet to slip.

verses.verse: For you, O God, have proved us; *
verses.halfverse: you have tried us just as silver is tried.

verses.verse: You brought us into the snare; *
verses.halfverse: you laid heavy burdens upon our backs.

verses.verse: You let enemies ride over our heads; we went through fire and water; *
verses.halfverse: but you brought us out into a place of refreshment.

verses.verse: I will enter your house with burnt-offerings and will pay you my vows, *
verses.halfverse: which I promised with my lips and spoke with my mouth when I was in trouble.

verses.verse: I will offer you sacrifices of fat beasts with the smoke of rams; *
verses.halfverse: I will give you oxen and goats.

verses.verse: Come and listen, all you who fear God, *
verses.halfverse: and I will tell you what he has done for me.

verses.verse: I called out to him with my mouth, *
verses.halfverse: and his praise was on my tongue.

verses.verse: If I had found evil in my heart, *
verses.halfverse: the Lord would not have heard me;

verses.verse: But in truth God has heard me; *
verses.halfverse: he has attended to the voice of my prayer.

verses.verse: Blessed be God, who has not rejected my prayer, *
verses.halfverse: nor withheld his love from me.

verses.verse: May God be merciful to us and bless us, *
verses.halfverse: show us the light of his countenance and come to us.

verses.verse: Let your ways be known upon earth, *
verses.halfverse: your saving health among all nations.

verses.verse: Let the peoples praise you, O God; *
verses.halfverse: let all the peoples praise you.

verses.verse: Let the nations be glad and sing for joy, *
verses.halfverse: for you judge the peoples with equity and guide all the nations upon earth.

verses.verse: Let the peoples praise you, O God; *
verses.halfverse: let all the peoples praise you.

verses.verse: The earth has brought forth her increase; *
verses.halfverse: may God, our own God, give us his blessing.

verses.verse: May God give us his blessing, *
verses.halfverse: and may all the ends of the earth stand in awe of him.

verses.verse: Let God arise, and let his enemies be scattered; *
verses.halfverse: let those who hate him flee before him.

verses.verse: Let them vanish like smoke when the wind drives it away; *
verses.halfverse: as the wax melts at the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.

verses.verse: But let the righteous be glad and rejoice before God; *
verses.halfverse: let them also be merry and joyful.

verses.verse: Sing to God, sing praises to his Name; exalt him who rides upon the heavens; *
verses.halfverse: YAHWEH is his Name, rejoice before him!

verses.verse: Father of orphans, defender of widows, *
verses.halfverse: God in his holy habitation!

verses.verse: God gives the solitary a home and brings forth prisoners into freedom; *
verses.halfverse: but the rebels shall live in dry places.

verses.verse: O God, when you went forth before your people, *
verses.halfverse: when you marched through the wilderness,

verses.verse: The earth shook, and the skies poured down rain, at the presence of God, the God of Sinai, *
verses.halfverse: at the presence of God, the God of Israel.

verses.verse: You sent a gracious rain, O God, upon your inheritance; *
verses.halfverse: you refreshed the land when it was weary.

verses.verse: Your people found their home in it; *
verses.halfverse: in your goodness, O God, you have made provision for the poor.

verses.verse: The Lord gave the word; *
verses.halfverse: great was the company of women who bore the tidings:

verses.verse: "Kings with their armies are fleeing away; *
verses.halfverse: the women at home are dividing the spoils."

verses.verse: Though you lingered among the sheepfolds, *
verses.halfverse: you shall be like a dove whose wings are covered with silver, whose feathers are like green gold.

verses.verse: When the Almighty scattered kings, *
verses.halfverse: it was like snow falling in Zalmon.

verses.verse: O mighty mountain, O hill of Bashan! *
verses.halfverse: O rugged mountain, O hill of Bashan!

verses.verse: Why do you look with envy, O rugged mountain, at the hill which God chose for his resting place? *
verses.halfverse: truly, the LORD will dwell there for ever.

verses.verse: The chariots of God are twenty thousand, even thousands of thousands; *
verses.halfverse: the Lord comes in holiness from Sinai.

verses.verse: You have gone up on high and led captivity captive; you have received gifts even from your enemies, *
verses.halfverse: that the LORD God might dwell among them.

verses.verse: Blessed be the Lord day by day, *
verses.halfverse: the God of our salvation, who bears our burdens.

verses.verse: He is our God, the God of our salvation; *
verses.halfverse: God is the LORD, by whom we escape death.

verses.verse: God shall crush the heads of his enemies, *
verses.halfverse: and the hairy scalp of those who go on still in their wickedness.

verses.verse: The Lord has said, "I will bring them back from Bashan; *
verses.halfverse: I will bring them back from the depths of the sea;

verses.verse: That your foot may be dipped in blood, *
verses.halfverse: the tongues of your dogs in the blood of your enemies."

verses.verse: They see your procession, O God, *
verses.halfverse: your procession into the sanctuary, my God and my King.

verses.verse: The singers go before, musicians follow after, *
verses.halfverse: in the midst of maidens playing upon the hand-drums.

verses.verse: Bless God in the congregation; *
verses.halfverse: bless the LORD, you that are of the fountain of Israel.

verses.verse: There is Benjamin, least of the tribes, at the head; the princes of Judah in a company; *
verses.halfverse: and the princes of Zebulon and Naphtali.

verses.verse: Send forth your strength, O God; *
verses.halfverse: establish, O God, what you have wrought for us.

verses.verse: Kings shall bring gifts to you, *
verses.halfverse: for your temple's sake at Jerusalem.

verses.verse: Rebuke the wild beast of the reeds, *
verses.halfverse: and the peoples, a herd of wild bulls with its calves.

verses.verse: Trample down those who lust after silver; *
verses.halfverse: scatter the peoples that delight in war.

verses.verse: Let tribute be brought out of Egypt; *
verses.halfverse: let Ethiopia stretch out her hands to God.

verses.verse: Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth; *
verses.halfverse: sing praises to the Lord.

verses.verse: He rides in the heavens, the ancient heavens; *
verses.halfverse: he sends forth his voice, his mighty voice.

verses.verse: Ascribe power to God; *
verses.halfverse: his majesty is over Israel; his strength is in the skies.

verses.verse: How wonderful is God in his holy places! *
verses.halfverse: the God of Israel giving strength and power to his people! Blessed be God!

verses.verse: Save me, O God, *
verses.halfverse: for the waters have risen up to my neck.

verses.verse: I am sinking in deep mire, *
verses.halfverse: and there is no firm ground for my feet.

verses.verse: I have come into deep waters, *
verses.halfverse: and the torrent washes over me.

verses.verse: I have grown weary with my crying; my throat is inflamed; *
verses.halfverse: my eyes have failed from looking for my God.

verses.verse: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head; my lying foes who would destroy me are mighty. *
verses.halfverse: Must I then give back what I never stole?

verses.verse: O God, you know my foolishness, *
verses.halfverse: and my faults are not hidden from you.

verses.verse: Let not those who hope in you be put to shame through me, Lord GOD of hosts; *
verses.halfverse: let not those who seek you be disgraced because of me, O God of Israel.

verses.verse: Surely, for your sake have I suffered reproach, *
verses.halfverse: and shame has covered my face.

verses.verse: I have become a stranger to my own kindred, *
verses.halfverse: an alien to my mother's children.

verses.verse: Zeal for your house has eaten me up; *
verses.halfverse: the scorn of those who scorn you has fallen upon me.

verses.verse: I humbled myself with fasting, *
verses.halfverse: but that was turned to my reproach.

verses.verse: I put on sack-cloth also, *
verses.halfverse: and became a byword among them.

verses.verse: Those who sit at the gate murmur against me, *
verses.halfverse: and the drunkards make songs about me.

verses.verse: But as for me, this is my prayer to you, *
verses.halfverse: at the time you have set, O LORD:

verses.verse: "In your great mercy, O God, *
verses.halfverse: answer me with your unfailing help.

verses.verse: Save me from the mire; do not let me sink; *
verses.halfverse: let me be rescued from those who hate me and out of the deep waters.

verses.verse: Let not the torrent of waters wash over me, neither let the deep swallow me up; *
verses.halfverse: do not let the Pit shut its mouth upon me.

verses.verse: Answer me, O LORD, for your love is kind; *
verses.halfverse: in your great compassion, turn to me."

verses.verse: "Hide not your face from your servant; *
verses.halfverse: be swift and answer me, for I am in distress.

verses.verse: Draw near to me and redeem me; *
verses.halfverse: because of my enemies deliver me.

verses.verse: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor; *
verses.halfverse: my adversaries are all in your sight."

verses.verse: Reproach has broken my heart, and it cannot be healed; *
verses.halfverse: I looked for sympathy, but there was none, for comforters, but I could find no one.

verses.verse: They gave me gall to eat, *
verses.halfverse: and when I was thirsty, they gave me vinegar to drink.

verses.verse: Let the table before them be a trap *
verses.halfverse: and their sacred feasts a snare.

verses.verse: Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, *
verses.halfverse: and give them continual trembling in their loins.

verses.verse: Pour out your indignation upon them, *
verses.halfverse: and let the fierceness of your anger overtake them.

verses.verse: Let their camp be desolate, *
verses.halfverse: and let there be none to dwell in their tents.

verses.verse: For they persecute him whom you have stricken *
verses.halfverse: and add to the pain of those whom you have pierced.

verses.verse: Lay to their charge guilt upon guilt, *
verses.halfverse: and let them not receive your vindication.

verses.verse: Let them be wiped out of the book of the living *
verses.halfverse: and not be written among the righteous.

verses.verse: As for me, I am afflicted and in pain; *
verses.halfverse: your help, O God, will lift me up on high.

verses.verse: I will praise the Name of God in song; *
verses.halfverse: I will proclaim his greatness with thanksgiving.

verses.verse: This will please the LORD more than an offering of oxen, *
verses.halfverse: more than bullocks with horns and hoofs.

verses.verse: The afflicted shall see and be glad; *
verses.halfverse: you who seek God, your heart shall live.

verses.verse: For the LORD listens to the needy, *
verses.halfverse: and his prisoners he does not despise.

verses.verse: Let the heavens and the earth praise him, *
verses.halfverse: the seas and all that moves in them;

verses.verse: For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah; *
verses.halfverse: they shall live there and have it in possession.

verses.verse: The children of his servants will inherit it, *
verses.halfverse: and those who love his Name will dwell therein.

verses.verse: Be pleased, O God, to deliver me; *
verses.halfverse: O LORD, make haste to help me.

verses.verse: Let those who seek my life be ashamed and altogether dismayed; *
verses.halfverse: let those who take pleasure in my misfortune draw back and be disgraced.

verses.verse: Let those who say to me "Aha!" and gloat over me turn back, *
verses.halfverse: because they are ashamed.

verses.verse: Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; *
verses.halfverse: let those who love your salvation say for ever, "Great is the LORD!"

verses.verse: But as for me, I am poor and needy; *
verses.halfverse: come to me speedily, O God.

verses.verse: You are my helper and my deliverer; *
verses.halfverse: O LORD, do not tarry.

verses.verse: In you, O LORD, have I taken refuge; *
verses.halfverse: let me never be ashamed.

verses.verse: In your righteousness, deliver me and set me free; *
verses.halfverse: incline your ear to me and save me.

verses.verse: Be my strong rock, a castle to keep me safe; *
verses.halfverse: you are my crag and my stronghold.

verses.verse: Deliver me, my God, from the hand of the wicked, *
verses.halfverse: from the clutches of the evildoer and the oppressor.

verses.verse: For you are my hope, O LORD God, *
verses.halfverse: my confidence since I was young.

verses.verse: I have been sustained by you ever since I was born; from my mother's womb you have been my strength; *
verses.halfverse: my praise shall be always of you.

verses.verse: I have become a portent to many; *
verses.halfverse: but you are my refuge and my strength.

verses.verse: Let my mouth be full of your praise *
verses.halfverse: and your glory all the day long.

verses.verse: Do not cast me off in my old age; *
verses.halfverse: forsake me not when my strength fails.

verses.verse: For my enemies are talking against me, *
verses.halfverse: and those who lie in wait for my life take counsel together.

verses.verse: They say, "God has forsaken him; go after him and seize him; *
verses.halfverse: because there is none who will save."

verses.verse: O God, be not far from me; *
verses.halfverse: come quickly to help me, O my God.

verses.verse: Let those who set themselves against me be put to shame and be disgraced; *
verses.halfverse: let those who seek to do me evil be covered with scorn and reproach.

verses.verse: But I shall always wait in patience, *
verses.halfverse: and shall praise you more and more.

verses.verse: My mouth shall recount your mighty acts and saving deeds all day long; *
verses.halfverse: though I cannot know the number of them.

verses.verse: I will begin with the mighty works of the Lord GOD; *
verses.halfverse: I will recall your righteousness, yours alone.

verses.verse: O God, you have taught me since I was young, *
verses.halfverse: and to this day I tell of your wonderful works.

verses.verse: And now that I am old and gray-headed, O God, do not forsake me, *
verses.halfverse: till I make known your strength to this generation and your power to all who are to come.

verses.verse: Your righteousness, O God, reaches to the heavens; *
verses.halfverse: you have done great things; who is like you, O God?

verses.verse: You have showed me great troubles and adversities, *
verses.halfverse: but you will restore my life and bring me up again from the deep places of the earth.

verses.verse: You strengthen me more and more; *
verses.halfverse: you enfold and comfort me,

verses.verse: Therefore I will praise you upon the lyre for your faithfulness, O my God; *
verses.halfverse: I will sing to you with the harp, O Holy One of Israel.

verses.verse: My lips will sing with joy when I play to you, *
verses.halfverse: and so will my soul, which you have redeemed.

verses.verse: My tongue will proclaim your righteousness all day long, *
verses.halfverse: for they are ashamed and disgraced who sought to do me harm.

verses.verse: Give the King your justice, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and your righteousness to the King's son;

verses.verse: That he may rule your people righteously *
verses.halfverse: and the poor with justice;

verses.verse: That the mountains may bring prosperity to the people, *
verses.halfverse: and the little hills bring righteousness.

verses.verse: He shall defend the needy among the people; *
verses.halfverse: he shall rescue the poor and crush the oppressor.

verses.verse: He shall live as long as the sun and moon endure, *
verses.halfverse: from one generation to another.

verses.verse: He shall come down like rain upon the mown field, *
verses.halfverse: like showers that water the earth.

verses.verse: In his time shall the righteous flourish; *
verses.halfverse: there shall be abundance of peace till the moon shall be no more.

verses.verse: He shall rule from sea to sea, *
verses.halfverse: and from the River to the ends of the earth.

verses.verse: His foes shall bow down before him, *
verses.halfverse: and his enemies lick the dust.

verses.verse: The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall pay tribute, *
verses.halfverse: and the kings of Arabia and Saba offer gifts.

verses.verse: All kings shall bow down before him, *
verses.halfverse: and all the nations do him service.

verses.verse: For he shall deliver the poor who cries out in distress, *
verses.halfverse: and the oppressed who has no helper.

verses.verse: He shall have pity on the lowly and poor; *
verses.halfverse: he shall preserve the lives of the needy.

verses.verse: He shall redeem their lives from oppression and violence, *
verses.halfverse: and dear shall their blood be in his sight.

verses.verse: Long may he live! and may there be given to him gold from Arabia; *
verses.halfverse: may prayer be made for him always, and may they bless him all the day long.

verses.verse: May there be abundance of grain on the earth, growing thick even on the hilltops; *
verses.halfverse: may its fruit flourish like Lebanon, and its grain like grass upon the earth.

verses.verse: May his Name remain for ever and be established as long as the sun endures; *
verses.halfverse: may all the nations bless themselves in him and call him blessed.

verses.verse: Blessed be the Lord GOD, the God of Israel, *
verses.halfverse: who alone does wondrous deeds!

verses.verse: And blessed be his glorious Name for ever! *
verses.halfverse: and may all the earth be filled with his glory. Amen. Amen.

verses.verse: Truly, God is good to Israel, *
verses.halfverse: to those who are pure in heart.

verses.verse: But as for me, my feet had nearly slipped; *
verses.halfverse: I had almost tripped and fallen;

verses.verse: Because I envied the proud *
verses.halfverse: and saw the prosperity of the wicked:

verses.verse: For they suffer no pain, *
verses.halfverse: and their bodies are sleek and sound;

verses.verse: In the misfortunes of others they have no share; *
verses.halfverse: they are not afflicted as others are;

verses.verse: Therefore they wear their pride like a necklace *
verses.halfverse: and wrap their violence about them like a cloak.

verses.verse: Their iniquity comes from gross minds, *
verses.halfverse: and their hearts overflow with wicked thoughts.

verses.verse: They scoff and speak maliciously; *
verses.halfverse: out of their haughtiness they plan oppression.

verses.verse: They set their mouths against the heavens, *
verses.halfverse: and their evil speech runs through the world.

verses.verse: And so the people turn to them *
verses.halfverse: and find in them no fault.

verses.verse: They say, "How should God know? *
verses.halfverse: is there knowledge in the Most High?"

verses.verse: So then, these are the wicked; *
verses.halfverse: always at ease, they increase their wealth.

verses.verse: In vain have I kept my heart clean, *
verses.halfverse: and washed my hands in innocence.

verses.verse: I have been afflicted all day long, *
verses.halfverse: and punished every morning.

verses.verse: Had I gone on speaking this way, *
verses.halfverse: I should have betrayed the generation of your children.

verses.verse: When I tried to understand these things, *
verses.halfverse: it was too hard for me;

verses.verse: Until I entered the sanctuary of God *
verses.halfverse: and discerned the end of the wicked.

verses.verse: Surely, you set them in slippery places; *
verses.halfverse: you cast them down in ruin.

verses.verse: Oh, how suddenly do they come to destruction, *
verses.halfverse: come to an end, and perish from terror!

verses.verse: Like a dream when one awakens, O Lord, *
verses.halfverse: when you arise you will make their image vanish.

verses.verse: When my mind became embittered, *
verses.halfverse: I was sorely wounded in my heart.

verses.verse: I was stupid and had no understanding; *
verses.halfverse: I was like a brute beast in your presence.

verses.verse: Yet I am always with you; *
verses.halfverse: you hold me by my right hand.

verses.verse: You will guide me by your counsel, *
verses.halfverse: and afterwards receive me with glory.

verses.verse: Whom have I in heaven but you? *
verses.halfverse: and having you I desire nothing upon earth.

verses.verse: Though my flesh and my heart should waste away, *
verses.halfverse: God is the strength of my heart and my portion for ever.

verses.verse: Truly, those who forsake you will perish; *
verses.halfverse: you destroy all who are unfaithful.

verses.verse: But it is good for me to be near God; *
verses.halfverse: I have made the Lord GOD my refuge.

verses.verse: I will speak of all your works *
verses.halfverse: in the gates of the city of Zion.

verses.verse: O God, why have you utterly cast us off? *
verses.halfverse: why is your wrath so hot against the sheep of your pasture?

verses.verse: Remember your congregation that you purchased long ago, *
verses.halfverse: the tribe you redeemed to be your inheritance, and Mount Zion where you dwell.

verses.verse: Turn your steps toward the endless ruins; *
verses.halfverse: the enemy has laid waste everything in your sanctuary.

verses.verse: Your adversaries roared in your holy place; *
verses.halfverse: they set up their banners as tokens of victory.

verses.verse: They were like men coming up with axes to a grove of trees; *
verses.halfverse: they broke down all your carved work with hatchets and hammers.

verses.verse: They set fire to your holy place; *
verses.halfverse: they defiled the dwelling-place of your Name and razed it to the ground.

verses.verse: They said to themselves, "Let us destroy them altogether." *
verses.halfverse: They burned down all the meeting-places of God in the land.

verses.verse: There are no signs for us to see; there is no prophet left; *
verses.halfverse: there is not one among us who knows how long.

verses.verse: How long, O God, will the adversary scoff? *
verses.halfverse: will the enemy blaspheme your Name for ever?

verses.verse: Why do you draw back your hand? *
verses.halfverse: why is your right hand hidden in your bosom?

verses.verse: Yet God is my King from ancient times, *
verses.halfverse: victorious in the midst of the earth.

verses.verse: You divided the sea by your might *
verses.halfverse: and shattered the heads of the dragons upon the waters;

verses.verse: You crushed the heads of Leviathan *
verses.halfverse: and gave him to the people of the desert for food.

verses.verse: You split open spring and torrent; *
verses.halfverse: you dried up ever-flowing rivers.

verses.verse: Yours is the day, yours also the night; *
verses.halfverse: you established the moon and the sun.

verses.verse: You fixed all the boundaries of the earth; *
verses.halfverse: you made both summer and winter.

verses.verse: Remember, O LORD, how the enemy scoffed, *
verses.halfverse: how a foolish people despised your Name.

verses.verse: Do not hand over the life of your dove to wild beasts; *
verses.halfverse: never forget the lives of your poor.

verses.verse: Look upon your covenant; *
verses.halfverse: the dark places of the earth are haunts of violence.

verses.verse: Let not the oppressed turn away ashamed; *
verses.halfverse: let the poor and needy praise your Name.

verses.verse: Arise, O God, maintain your cause; *
verses.halfverse: remember how fools revile you all day long.

verses.verse: Forget not the clamor of your adversaries, *
verses.halfverse: the unending tumult of those who rise up against you.

verses.verse: We give you thanks, O God, we give you thanks, *
verses.halfverse: calling upon your Name and declaring all your wonderful deeds.

verses.verse: "I will appoint a time," says God; *
verses.halfverse: "I will judge with equity.

verses.verse: Though the earth and all its inhabitants are quaking, *
verses.halfverse: I will make its pillars fast.

verses.verse: I will say to the boasters, 'Boast no more,' *
verses.halfverse: and to the wicked, 'Do not toss your horns;

verses.verse: Do not toss your horns so high, *
verses.halfverse: nor speak with a proud neck.'"

verses.verse: For judgment is neither from the east nor from the west, *
verses.halfverse: nor yet from the wilderness or the mountains.

verses.verse: It is God who judges; *
verses.halfverse: he puts down one and lifts up another.

verses.verse: For in the LORD'S hand there is a cup, full of spiced and foaming wine, which he pours out, *
verses.halfverse: and all the wicked of the earth shall drink and drain the dregs.

verses.verse: But I will rejoice for ever; *
verses.halfverse: I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

verses.verse: He shall break off all the horns of the wicked; *
verses.halfverse: but the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

verses.verse: In Judah is God known; *
verses.halfverse: his Name is great in Israel.

verses.verse: At Salem is his tabernacle, *
verses.halfverse: and his dwelling is in Zion.

verses.verse: There he broke the flashing arrows, *
verses.halfverse: the shield, the sword, and the weapons of battle.

verses.verse: How glorious you are! *
verses.halfverse: more splendid than the everlasting mountains!

verses.verse: The strong of heart have been despoiled; they sink into sleep; *
verses.halfverse: none of the warriors can lift a hand.

verses.verse: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, *
verses.halfverse: both horse and rider lie stunned.

verses.verse: What terror you inspire! *
verses.halfverse: who can stand before you when you are angry?

verses.verse: From heaven you pronounced judgment; *
verses.halfverse: the earth was afraid and was still;

verses.verse: When God rose up to judgment *
verses.halfverse: and to save all the oppressed of the earth.

verses.verse: Truly, wrathful Edom will give you thanks, *
verses.halfverse: and the remnant of Hamath will keep your feasts.

verses.verse: Make a vow to the LORD your God and keep it; *
verses.halfverse: let all around him bring gifts to him who is worthy to be feared.

verses.verse: He breaks the spirit of princes, *
verses.halfverse: and strikes terror in the kings of the earth.

verses.verse: I will cry aloud to God; *
verses.halfverse: I will cry aloud, and he will hear me.

verses.verse: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; *
verses.halfverse: my hands were stretched out by night and did not tire; I refused to be comforted.

verses.verse: I think of God, I am restless, *
verses.halfverse: I ponder, and my spirit faints.

verses.verse: You will not let my eyelids close; *
verses.halfverse: I am troubled and I cannot speak.

verses.verse: I consider the days of old; *
verses.halfverse: I remember the years long past;

verses.verse: I commune with my heart in the night; *
verses.halfverse: I ponder and search my mind.

verses.verse: Will the Lord cast me off for ever? *
verses.halfverse: will he no more show his favor?

verses.verse: Has his loving-kindness come to an end for ever? *
verses.halfverse: has his promise failed for evermore?

verses.verse: Has God forgotten to be gracious? *
verses.halfverse: has he, in his anger, withheld his compassion?

verses.verse: And I said, "My grief is this: *
verses.halfverse: the right hand of the Most High has lost its power."

verses.verse: I will remember the works of the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and call to mind your wonders of old time.

verses.verse: I will meditate on all your acts *
verses.halfverse: and ponder your mighty deeds.

verses.verse: Your way, O God, is holy; *
verses.halfverse: who is so great a god as our God?

verses.verse: You are the God who works wonders *
verses.halfverse: and have declared your power among the peoples.

verses.verse: By your strength you have redeemed your people, *
verses.halfverse: the children of Jacob and Joseph.

verses.verse: The waters saw you, O God; the waters saw you and trembled; *
verses.halfverse: the very depths were shaken.

verses.verse: The clouds poured out water; the skies thundered; *
verses.halfverse: your arrows flashed to and fro;

verses.verse: The sound of your thunder was in the whirlwind; your lightnings lit up the world; *
verses.halfverse: the earth trembled and shook.

verses.verse: Your way was in the sea, and your paths in the great waters, *
verses.halfverse: yet your footsteps were not seen.

verses.verse: You led your people like a flock *
verses.halfverse: by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

verses.verse: Hear my teaching, O my people; *
verses.halfverse: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

verses.verse: I will open my mouth in a parable; *
verses.halfverse: I will declare the mysteries of ancient times.

verses.verse: That which we have heard and known, and what our forefathers have told us, *
verses.halfverse: we will not hide from their children.

verses.verse: We will recount to generations to come the praiseworthy deeds and the power of the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and the wonderful works he has done.

verses.verse: He gave his decrees to Jacob and established a law for Israel, *
verses.halfverse: which he commanded them to teach their children;

verses.verse: That the generations to come might know, and the children yet unborn; *
verses.halfverse: that they in their turn might tell it to their children;

verses.verse: So that they might put their trust in God, *
verses.halfverse: and not forget the deeds of God, but keep his commandments;

verses.verse: And not be like their forefathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, *
verses.halfverse: a generation whose heart was not steadfast, and whose spirit was not faithful to God.

verses.verse: The people of Ephraim, armed with the bow, *
verses.halfverse: turned back in the day of battle;

verses.verse: They did not keep the covenant of God, *
verses.halfverse: and refused to walk in his law;

verses.verse: They forgot what he had done, *
verses.halfverse: and the wonders he had shown them.

verses.verse: He worked marvels in the sight of their forefathers, *
verses.halfverse: in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.

verses.verse: He split open the sea and let them pass through; *
verses.halfverse: he made the waters stand up like walls.

verses.verse: He led them with a cloud by day, *
verses.halfverse: and all the night through with a glow of fire.

verses.verse: He split the hard rocks in the wilderness *
verses.halfverse: and gave them drink as from the great deep.

verses.verse: He brought streams out of the cliff, *
verses.halfverse: and the waters gushed out like rivers.

verses.verse: But they went on sinning against him, *
verses.halfverse: rebelling in the desert against the Most High.

verses.verse: They tested God in their hearts, *
verses.halfverse: demanding food for their craving.

verses.verse: They railed against God and said, *
verses.halfverse: "Can God set a table in the wilderness?

verses.verse: True, he struck the rock, the waters gushed out, and the gullies overflowed; *
verses.halfverse: but is he able to give bread or to provide meat for his people?"

verses.verse: When the LORD heard this, he was full of wrath; *
verses.halfverse: a fire was kindled against Jacob, and his anger mounted against Israel;

verses.verse: For they had no faith in God, *
verses.halfverse: nor did they put their trust in his saving power.

verses.verse: So he commanded the clouds above *
verses.halfverse: and opened the doors of heaven.

verses.verse: He rained down manna upon them to eat *
verses.halfverse: and gave them grain from heaven.

verses.verse: So mortals ate the bread of angels; *
verses.halfverse: he provided for them food enough.

verses.verse: He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens *
verses.halfverse: and led out the south wind by his might.

verses.verse: He rained down flesh upon them like dust *
verses.halfverse: and wingèd birds like the sand of the sea.

verses.verse: He let it fall in the midst of their camp *
verses.halfverse: and round about their dwellings.

verses.verse: So they ate and were well filled, *
verses.halfverse: for he gave them what they craved.

verses.verse: But they did not stop their craving, *
verses.halfverse: though the food was still in their mouths.

verses.verse: So God's anger mounted against them; *
verses.halfverse: he slew their strongest men and laid low the youth of Israel.

verses.verse: In spite of all this, they went on sinning *
verses.halfverse: and had no faith in his wonderful works.

verses.verse: So he brought their days to an end like a breath *
verses.halfverse: and their years in sudden terror.

verses.verse: Whenever he slew them, they would seek him, *
verses.halfverse: and repent, and diligently search for God.

verses.verse: They would remember that God was their rock, *
verses.halfverse: and the Most High God their redeemer.

verses.verse: But they flattered him with their mouths *
verses.halfverse: and lied to him with their tongues.

verses.verse: Their heart was not steadfast toward him, *
verses.halfverse: and they were not faithful to his covenant.

verses.verse: But he was so merciful that he forgave their sins and did not destroy them; *
verses.halfverse: many times he held back his anger and did not permit his wrath to be roused.

verses.verse: For he remembered that they were but flesh, *
verses.halfverse: a breath that goes forth and does not return.

verses.verse: How often the people disobeyed him in the wilderness *
verses.halfverse: and offended him in the desert!

verses.verse: Again and again they tempted God *
verses.halfverse: and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

verses.verse: They did not remember his power *
verses.halfverse: in the day when he ransomed them from the enemy;

verses.verse: How he wrought his signs in Egypt *
verses.halfverse: and his omens in the field of Zoan.

verses.verse: He turned their rivers into blood, *
verses.halfverse: so that they could not drink of their streams.

verses.verse: He sent swarms of flies among them, which ate them up, *
verses.halfverse: and frogs, which destroyed them.

verses.verse: He gave their crops to the caterpillar, *
verses.halfverse: the fruit of their toil to the locust.

verses.verse: He killed their vines with hail *
verses.halfverse: and their sycamores with frost.

verses.verse: He delivered their cattle to hailstones *
verses.halfverse: and their livestock to hot thunderbolts.

verses.verse: He poured out upon them his blazing anger: *
verses.halfverse: fury, indignation, and distress, a troop of destroying angels.

verses.verse: He gave full rein to his anger; he did not spare their souls from death; *
verses.halfverse: but delivered their lives to the plague.

verses.verse: He struck down all the firstborn of Egypt, *
verses.halfverse: the flower of manhood in the dwellings of Ham.

verses.verse: He led out his people like sheep *
verses.halfverse: and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

verses.verse: He led them to safety, and they were not afraid; *
verses.halfverse: but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.

verses.verse: He brought them to his holy land, *
verses.halfverse: the mountain his right hand had won.

verses.verse: He drove out the Canaanites before them and apportioned an inheritance to them by lot; *
verses.halfverse: he made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

verses.verse: But they tested the Most High God, and defied him, *
verses.halfverse: and did not keep his commandments.

verses.verse: They turned away and were disloyal like their fathers; *
verses.halfverse: they were undependable like a warped bow.

verses.verse: They grieved him with their hill-altars *
verses.halfverse: they provoked his displeasure with their idols.

verses.verse: When God heard this, he was angry *
verses.halfverse: and utterly rejected Israel.

verses.verse: He forsook the shrine at Shiloh, *
verses.halfverse: the tabernacle where he had lived among his people.

verses.verse: He delivered the ark into captivity, *
verses.halfverse: his glory into the adversary's hand.

verses.verse: He gave his people to the sword *
verses.halfverse: and was angered against his inheritance.

verses.verse: The fire consumed their young men; *
verses.halfverse: there were no wedding songs for their maidens.

verses.verse: Their priests fell by the sword, *
verses.halfverse: and their widows made no lamentation.

verses.verse: Then the LORD woke as though from sleep, *
verses.halfverse: like a warrior refreshed with wine.

verses.verse: He struck his enemies on the backside *
verses.halfverse: and put them to perpetual shame.

verses.verse: He rejected the tent of Joseph *
verses.halfverse: and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim;

verses.verse: He chose instead the tribe of Judah *
verses.halfverse: and Mount Zion, which he loved.
