verses.verse: He built his sanctuary like the heights of heaven, *
verses.halfverse: like the earth which he founded for ever.

verses.verse: He chose David his servant, *
verses.halfverse: and took him away from the sheepfolds.

verses.verse: He brought him from following the ewes, *
verses.halfverse: to be a shepherd over Jacob his people and over Israel his inheritance.

verses.verse: So he shepherded them with a faithful and true heart *
verses.halfverse: and guided them with the skillfulness of his hands.

verses.verse: O God, the heathen have come into your inheritance; they have profaned your holy temple; *
verses.halfverse: they have made Jerusalem a heap of rubble.

verses.verse: They have given the bodies of your servants as food for the birds of the air, *
verses.halfverse: and the flesh of your faithful ones to the beasts of the field.

verses.verse: They have shed their blood like water on every side of Jerusalem, *
verses.halfverse: and there was no one to bury them.

verses.verse: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, *
verses.halfverse: an object of scorn and derision to those around us.

verses.verse: How long will you be angry, O LORD? *
verses.halfverse: will your fury blaze like fire for ever?

verses.verse: Pour out your wrath upon the heathen who have not known you *
verses.halfverse: and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon your Name.

verses.verse: For they have devoured Jacob *
verses.halfverse: and made his dwelling a ruin.

verses.verse: Remember not our past sins; let your compassion be swift to meet us; *
verses.halfverse: for we have been brought very low.

verses.verse: Help us, O God our Savior, for the glory of your Name; *
verses.halfverse: deliver us and forgive us our sins, for your Name's sake.

verses.verse: Why should the heathen say, "Where is their God?" *
verses.halfverse: Let it be known among the heathen and in our sight that you avenge the shedding of your servants' blood.

verses.verse: Let the sorrowful sighing of the prisoners come before you, *
verses.halfverse: and by your great might spare those who are condemned to die.

verses.verse: May the revilings with which they reviled you, O Lord, *
verses.halfverse: return seven-fold into their bosoms.

verses.verse: For we are your people and the sheep of your pasture; *
verses.halfverse: we will give you thanks for ever and show forth your praise from age to age.

verses.verse: Hear, O Shepherd of Israel, leading Joseph like a flock; *
verses.halfverse: shine forth, you that are enthroned upon the cherubim.

verses.verse: In the presence of Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh, *
verses.halfverse: stir up your strength and come to help us.

verses.verse: Restore us, O God of hosts; *
verses.halfverse: show the light of your countenance, and we shall be saved.

verses.verse: O LORD God of hosts, *
verses.halfverse: how long will you be angered despite the prayers of your people?

verses.verse: You have fed them with the bread of tears; *
verses.halfverse: you have given them bowls of tears to drink.

verses.verse: You have made us the derision of our neighbors, *
verses.halfverse: and our enemies laugh us to scorn.

verses.verse: Restore us, O God of hosts; *
verses.halfverse: show the light of your countenance, and we shall be saved.

verses.verse: You have brought a vine out of Egypt; *
verses.halfverse: you cast out the nations and planted it.

verses.verse: You prepared the ground for it; *
verses.halfverse: it took root and filled the land.

verses.verse: The mountains were covered by its shadow *
verses.halfverse: and the towering cedar trees by its boughs.

verses.verse: You stretched out its tendrils to the Sea *
verses.halfverse: and its branches to the River.

verses.verse: Why have you broken down its wall, *
verses.halfverse: so that all who pass by pluck off its grapes?

verses.verse: The wild boar of the forest has ravaged it, *
verses.halfverse: and the beasts of the field have grazed upon it.

verses.verse: Turn now, O God of hosts, look down from heaven; behold and tend this vine; *
verses.halfverse: preserve what your right hand has planted.

verses.verse: They burn it with fire like rubbish; *
verses.halfverse: at the rebuke of your countenance let them perish.

verses.verse: Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, *
verses.halfverse: the son of man you have made so strong for yourself.

verses.verse: And so will we never turn away from you; *
verses.halfverse: give us life, that we may call upon your Name.

verses.verse: Restore us, O LORD God of hosts; *
verses.halfverse: show the light of your countenance, and we shall be saved.

verses.verse: Sing with joy to God our strength *
verses.halfverse: and raise a loud shout to the God of Jacob.

verses.verse: Raise a song and sound the timbrel, *
verses.halfverse: the merry harp, and the lyre.

verses.verse: Blow the ram's-horn at the new moon, *
verses.halfverse: and at the full moon, the day of our feast.

verses.verse: For this is a statute for Israel, *
verses.halfverse: a law of the God of Jacob.

verses.verse: He laid it as a solemn charge upon Joseph, *
verses.halfverse: when he came out of the land of Egypt.

verses.verse: I heard an unfamiliar voice saying, *
verses.halfverse: "I eased his shoulder from the burden; his hands were set free from bearing the load."

verses.verse: You called on me in trouble, and I saved you; *
verses.halfverse: I answered you from the secret place of thunder and tested you at the waters of Meribah.

verses.verse: Hear, O my people, and I will admonish you: *
verses.halfverse: O Israel, if you would but listen to me!

verses.verse: There shall be no strange god among you; *
verses.halfverse: you shall not worship a foreign god.

verses.verse: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt and said, *
verses.halfverse: "Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it."

verses.verse: And yet my people did not hear my voice, *
verses.halfverse: and Israel would not obey me.

verses.verse: So I gave them over to the stubbornness of their hearts, *
verses.halfverse: to follow their own devices.

verses.verse: Oh, that my people would listen to me! *
verses.halfverse: that Israel would walk in my ways!

verses.verse: I should soon subdue their enemies *
verses.halfverse: and turn my hand against their foes.

verses.verse: Those who hate the LORD would cringe before him, *
verses.halfverse: and their punishment would last for ever.

verses.verse: But Israel would I feed with the finest wheat *
verses.halfverse: and satisfy him with honey from the rock.

verses.verse: God takes his stand in the council of heaven; *
verses.halfverse: he gives judgment in the midst of the gods:

verses.verse: "How long will you judge unjustly, *
verses.halfverse: and show favor to the wicked?

verses.verse: Save the weak and the orphan; *
verses.halfverse: defend the humble and needy;

verses.verse: Rescue the weak and the poor; *
verses.halfverse: deliver them from the power of the wicked.

verses.verse: They do not know, neither do they understand; they go about in darkness; *
verses.halfverse: all the foundations of the earth are shaken.

verses.verse: Now I say to you, 'You are gods, *
verses.halfverse: and all of you children of the Most High;

verses.verse: Nevertheless, you shall die like mortals, *
verses.halfverse: and fall like any prince.'"

verses.verse: Arise, O God, and rule the earth, *
verses.halfverse: for you shall take all nations for your own.

verses.verse: O God, do not be silent; *
verses.halfverse: do not keep still nor hold your peace, O God;

verses.verse: For your enemies are in tumult, *
verses.halfverse: and those who hate you have lifted up their heads.

verses.verse: They take secret counsel against your people *
verses.halfverse: and plot against those whom you protect.

verses.verse: They have said, "Come, let us wipe them out from among the nations; *
verses.halfverse: let the name of Israel be remembered no more."

verses.verse: They have conspired together; *
verses.halfverse: they have made an alliance against you:

verses.verse: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites; *
verses.halfverse: the Moabites and the Hagarenes;

verses.verse: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; *
verses.halfverse: the Philistines and those who dwell in Tyre.

verses.verse: The Assyrians also have joined them, *
verses.halfverse: and have come to help the people of Lot.

verses.verse: Do to them as you did to Midian, *
verses.halfverse: to Sisera, and to Jabin at the river of Kishon:

verses.verse: They were destroyed at Endor; *
verses.halfverse: they became like dung upon the ground.

verses.verse: Make their leaders like Oreb and Zeëb, *
verses.halfverse: and all their commanders like Zebah and Zalmunna,

verses.verse: Who said, "Let us take for ourselves *
verses.halfverse: the fields of God as our possession."

verses.verse: O my God, make them like whirling dust *
verses.halfverse: and like chaff before the wind;

verses.verse: Like fire that burns down a forest, *
verses.halfverse: like the flame that sets mountains ablaze.

verses.verse: Drive them with your tempest *
verses.halfverse: and terrify them with your storm;

verses.verse: Cover their faces with shame, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: that they may seek your Name.

verses.verse: Let them be disgraced and terrified for ever; *
verses.halfverse: let them be put to confusion and perish.

verses.verse: Let them know that you, whose Name is YAHWEH, *
verses.halfverse: you alone are the Most High over all the earth.

verses.verse: How dear to me is your dwelling, O LORD of hosts! *
verses.halfverse: My soul has a desire and longing for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh rejoice in the living God.

verses.verse: The sparrow has found her a house and the swallow a nest where she may lay her young; *
verses.halfverse: by the side of your altars, O LORD of hosts, my King and my God.

verses.verse: Happy are they who dwell in your house! *
verses.halfverse: they will always be praising you.

verses.verse: Happy are the people whose strength is in you! *
verses.halfverse: whose hearts are set on the pilgrims' way.

verses.verse: Those who go through the desolate valley will find it a place of springs, *
verses.halfverse: for the early rains have covered it with pools of water.

verses.verse: They will climb from height to height, *
verses.halfverse: and the God of gods will reveal himself in Zion.

verses.verse: LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; *
verses.halfverse: hearken, O God of Jacob.

verses.verse: Behold our defender, O God; *
verses.halfverse: and look upon the face of your Anointed.

verses.verse: For one day in your courts is better than a thousand in my own room, *
verses.halfverse: and to stand at the threshold of the house of my God than to dwell in the tents of the wicked.

verses.verse: For the LORD God is both sun and shield; *
verses.halfverse: he will give grace and glory;

verses.verse: No good thing will the LORD withhold *
verses.halfverse: from those who walk with integrity.

verses.verse: O LORD of hosts, *
verses.halfverse: happy are they who put their trust in you!

verses.verse: You have been gracious to your land, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: you have restored the good fortune of Jacob.

verses.verse: You have forgiven the iniquity of your people *
verses.halfverse: and blotted out all their sins.

verses.verse: You have withdrawn all your fury *
verses.halfverse: and turned yourself from your wrathful indignation.

verses.verse: Restore us then, O God our Savior; *
verses.halfverse: let your anger depart from us.

verses.verse: Will you be displeased with us for ever? *
verses.halfverse: will you prolong your anger from age to age?

verses.verse: Will you not give us life again, *
verses.halfverse: that your people may rejoice in you?

verses.verse: Show us your mercy, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and grant us your salvation.

verses.verse: I will listen to what the LORD God is saying, *
verses.halfverse: for he is speaking peace to his faithful people and to those who turn their hearts to him.

verses.verse: Truly, his salvation is very near to those who fear him, *
verses.halfverse: that his glory may dwell in our land.

verses.verse: Mercy and truth have met together; *
verses.halfverse: righteousness and peace have kissed each other.

verses.verse: Truth shall spring up from the earth, *
verses.halfverse: and righteousness shall look down from heaven.

verses.verse: The LORD will indeed grant prosperity, *
verses.halfverse: and our land will yield its increase.

verses.verse: Righteousness shall go before him, *
verses.halfverse: and peace shall be a pathway for his feet.

verses.verse: Bow down your ear, O LORD, and answer me, *
verses.halfverse: for I am poor and in misery.

verses.verse: Keep watch over my life, for I am faithful; *
verses.halfverse: save your servant who puts his trust in you.

verses.verse: Be merciful to me, O LORD, for you are my God; *
verses.halfverse: I call upon you all the day long.

verses.verse: Gladden the soul of your servant, *
verses.halfverse: for to you, O LORD, I lift up my soul.

verses.verse: For you, O LORD, are good and forgiving, *
verses.halfverse: and great is your love toward all who call upon you.

verses.verse: Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer, *
verses.halfverse: and attend to the voice of my supplications.

verses.verse: In the time of my trouble I will call upon you, *
verses.halfverse: for you will answer me.

verses.verse: Among the gods there is none like you, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: nor anything like your works.

verses.verse: All the nations you have made will come and worship you, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and glorify your Name.

verses.verse: For you are great; you do wondrous things; *
verses.halfverse: and you alone are God.

verses.verse: Teach me your way, O LORD, and I will walk in your truth; *
verses.halfverse: knit my heart to you that I may fear your Name.

verses.verse: I will thank you, O LORD my God, with all my heart, *
verses.halfverse: and glorify your Name for evermore.

verses.verse: For great is your love toward me; *
verses.halfverse: you have delivered me from the nethermost Pit.

verses.verse: The arrogant rise up against me, O God, and a band of violent men seeks my life; *
verses.halfverse: they have not set you before their eyes.

verses.verse: But you, O LORD, are gracious and full of compassion, *
verses.halfverse: slow to anger, and full of kindness and truth.

verses.verse: Turn to me and have mercy upon me; *
verses.halfverse: give your strength to your servant; and save the child of your handmaid.

verses.verse: Show me a sign of your favor, so that those who hate me may see it and be ashamed; *
verses.halfverse: because you, O LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

verses.verse: On the holy mountain stands the city he has founded; *
verses.halfverse: the LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

verses.verse: Glorious things are spoken of you, *
verses.halfverse: O city of our God.

verses.verse: I count Egypt and Babylon among those who know me; *
verses.halfverse: behold Philistia, Tyre, and Ethiopia: in Zion were they born.

verses.verse: Of Zion it shall be said, "Everyone was born in her, *
verses.halfverse: and the Most High himself shall sustain her."

verses.verse: The LORD will record as he enrolls the peoples, *
verses.halfverse: "These also were born there."

verses.verse: The singers and the dancers will say, *
verses.halfverse: "All my fresh springs are in you."

verses.verse: O LORD, my God, my Savior, *
verses.halfverse: by day and night I cry to you.

verses.verse: Let my prayer enter into your presence; *
verses.halfverse: incline your ear to my lamentation.

verses.verse: For I am full of trouble; *
verses.halfverse: my life is at the brink of the grave.

verses.verse: I am counted among those who go down to the Pit; *
verses.halfverse: I have become like one who has no strength;

verses.verse: Lost among the dead, *
verses.halfverse: like the slain who lie in the grave,

verses.verse: Whom you remember no more, *
verses.halfverse: for they are cut off from your hand.

verses.verse: You have laid me in the depths of the Pit, *
verses.halfverse: in dark places, and in the abyss.

verses.verse: Your anger weighs upon me heavily, *
verses.halfverse: and all your great waves overwhelm me.

verses.verse: You have put my friends far from me; you have made me to be abhorred by them; *
verses.halfverse: I am in prison and cannot get free.

verses.verse: My sight has failed me because of trouble; *
verses.halfverse: LORD, I have called upon you daily; I have stretched out my hands to you.

verses.verse: Do you work wonders for the dead? *
verses.halfverse: will those who have died stand up and give you thanks?

verses.verse: Will your loving-kindness be declared in the grave? *
verses.halfverse: your faithfulness in the land of destruction?

verses.verse: Will your wonders be known in the dark? *
verses.halfverse: or your righteousness in the country where all is forgotten?

verses.verse: But as for me, O LORD, I cry to you for help; *
verses.halfverse: in the morning my prayer comes before you.

verses.verse: LORD, why have you rejected me? *
verses.halfverse: why have you hidden your face from me?

verses.verse: Ever since my youth, I have been wretched and at the point of death; *
verses.halfverse: I have borne your terrors with a troubled mind.

verses.verse: Your blazing anger has swept over me; *
verses.halfverse: your terrors have destroyed me;

verses.verse: They surround me all day long like a flood; *
verses.halfverse: they encompass me on every side.

verses.verse: My friend and my neighbor you have put away from me, *
verses.halfverse: and darkness is my only companion.

verses.verse: Your love, O LORD, for ever will I sing; *
verses.halfverse: from age to age my mouth will proclaim your faithfulness.

verses.verse: For I am persuaded that your love is established for ever; *
verses.halfverse: you have set your faithfulness firmly in the heavens.

verses.verse: "I have made a covenant with my chosen one; *
verses.halfverse: I have sworn an oath to David my servant:

verses.verse: 'I will establish your line for ever, *
verses.halfverse: and preserve your throne for all generations.'"

verses.verse: The heavens bear witness to your wonders, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and to your faithfulness in the assembly of the holy ones;

verses.verse: For who in the skies can be compared to the LORD? *
verses.halfverse: who is like the LORD among the gods?

verses.verse: God is much to be feared in the council of the holy ones, *
verses.halfverse: great and terrible to all those round about him.

verses.verse: Who is like you, LORD God of hosts? *
verses.halfverse: O mighty LORD, your faithfulness is all around you.

verses.verse: You rule the raging of the sea *
verses.halfverse: and still the surging of its waves.

verses.verse: You have crushed Rahab of the deep with a deadly wound; *
verses.halfverse: you have scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.

verses.verse: Yours are the heavens; the earth also is yours; *
verses.halfverse: you laid the foundations of the world and all that is in it.

verses.verse: You have made the north and the south; *
verses.halfverse: Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your Name.

verses.verse: You have a mighty arm; *
verses.halfverse: strong is your hand and high is your right hand.

verses.verse: Righteousness and justice are the foundations of your throne; *
verses.halfverse: love and truth go before your face.

verses.verse: Happy are the people who know the festal shout! *
verses.halfverse: they walk, O LORD, in the light of your presence.

verses.verse: They rejoice daily in your Name; *
verses.halfverse: they are jubilant in your righteousness.

verses.verse: For you are the glory of their strength, *
verses.halfverse: and by your favor our might is exalted.

verses.verse: Truly, the LORD is our ruler; *
verses.halfverse: The Holy One of Israel is our King.

verses.verse: You spoke once in a vision and said to your faithful people: *
verses.halfverse: "I have set the crown upon a warrior and have exalted one chosen out of the people.

verses.verse: I have found David my servant; *
verses.halfverse: with my holy oil have I anointed him.

verses.verse: My hand will hold him fast *
verses.halfverse: and my arm will make him strong.

verses.verse: No enemy shall deceive him, *
verses.halfverse: nor any wicked man bring him down.

verses.verse: I will crush his foes before him *
verses.halfverse: and strike down those who hate him.

verses.verse: My faithfulness and love shall be with him, *
verses.halfverse: and he shall be victorious through my Name.

verses.verse: I shall make his dominion extend *
verses.halfverse: from the Great Sea to the River.

verses.verse: He will say to me, 'You are my Father, *
verses.halfverse: my God, and the rock of my salvation.'

verses.verse: I will make him my firstborn *
verses.halfverse: and higher than the kings of the earth.

verses.verse: I will keep my love for him for ever, *
verses.halfverse: and my covenant will stand firm for him.

verses.verse: I will establish his line for ever *
verses.halfverse: and his throne as the days of heaven."

verses.verse: "If his children forsake my law *
verses.halfverse: and do not walk according to my judgments;

verses.verse: If they break my statutes *
verses.halfverse: and do not keep my commandments;

verses.verse: I will punish their transgressions with a rod *
verses.halfverse: and their iniquities with the lash;

verses.verse: But I will not take my love from him, *
verses.halfverse: nor let my faithfulness prove false.

verses.verse: I will not break my covenant, *
verses.halfverse: nor change what has gone out of my lips.

verses.verse: Once for all I have sworn by my holiness: *
verses.halfverse: 'I will not lie to David.

verses.verse: His line shall endure for ever *
verses.halfverse: and his throne as the sun before me;

verses.verse: It shall stand fast for evermore like the moon, *
verses.halfverse: the abiding witness in the sky.'"

verses.verse: But you have cast off and rejected your anointed; *
verses.halfverse: you have become enraged at him.

verses.verse: You have broken your covenant with your servant, *
verses.halfverse: defiled his crown, and hurled it to the ground.

verses.verse: You have breached all his walls *
verses.halfverse: and laid his strongholds in ruins.

verses.verse: All who pass by despoil him; *
verses.halfverse: he has become the scorn of his neighbors.

verses.verse: You have exalted the right hand of his foes *
verses.halfverse: and made all his enemies rejoice.

verses.verse: You have turned back the edge of his sword *
verses.halfverse: and have not sustained him in battle.

verses.verse: You have put an end to his splendor *
verses.halfverse: and cast his throne to the ground.

verses.verse: You have cut short the days of his youth *
verses.halfverse: and have covered him with shame.

verses.verse: How long will you hide yourself, O LORD? will you hide yourself for ever? *
verses.halfverse: how long will your anger burn like fire?

verses.verse: Remember, LORD, how short life is, *
verses.halfverse: how frail you have made all flesh.

verses.verse: Who can live and not see death? *
verses.halfverse: who can save himself from the power of the grave?

verses.verse: Where, Lord, are your loving-kindnesses of old, *
verses.halfverse: which you promised David in your faithfulness?

verses.verse: Remember, Lord, how your servant is mocked, *
verses.halfverse: how I carry in my bosom the taunts of many peoples,

verses.verse: The taunts your enemies have hurled, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: which they hurled at the heels of your anointed.

verses.verse: Blessed be the LORD for evermore! *
verses.halfverse: Amen, I say, Amen.

verses.verse: Lord, you have been our refuge *
verses.halfverse: from one generation to another.

verses.verse: Before the mountains were brought forth, or the land and the earth were born, *
verses.halfverse: from age to age you are God.

verses.verse: You turn us back to the dust and say, *
verses.halfverse: "Go back, O child of earth."

verses.verse: For a thousand years in your sight are like yesterday when it is past *
verses.halfverse: and like a watch in the night.

verses.verse: You sweep us away like a dream; *
verses.halfverse: we fade away suddenly like the grass.

verses.verse: In the morning it is green and flourishes; *
verses.halfverse: in the evening it is dried up and withered.

verses.verse: For we consume away in your displeasure; *
verses.halfverse: we are afraid because of your wrathful indignation.

verses.verse: Our iniquities you have set before you, *
verses.halfverse: and our secret sins in the light of your countenance.

verses.verse: When you are angry, all our days are gone; *
verses.halfverse: we bring our years to an end like a sigh.

verses.verse: The span of our life is seventy years, perhaps in strength even eighty; *
verses.halfverse: yet the sum of them is but labor and sorrow, for they pass away quickly and we are gone.

verses.verse: Who regards the power of your wrath? *
verses.halfverse: who rightly fears your indignation?

verses.verse: So teach us to number our days *
verses.halfverse: that we may apply our hearts to wisdom.

verses.verse: Return, O LORD; how long will you tarry? *
verses.halfverse: be gracious to your servants.

verses.verse: Satisfy us by your loving-kindness in the morning; *
verses.halfverse: so shall we rejoice and be glad all the days of our life.

verses.verse: Make us glad by the measure of the days that you afflicted us *
verses.halfverse: and the years in which we suffered adversity.

verses.verse: Show your servants your works *
verses.halfverse: and your splendor to their children.

verses.verse: May the graciousness of the LORD our God be upon us; *
verses.halfverse: prosper the work of our hands; prosper our handiwork.

verses.verse: He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High, *
verses.halfverse: abides under the shadow of the Almighty.

verses.verse: He shall say to the LORD, "You are my refuge and my stronghold, *
verses.halfverse: my God in whom I put my trust."

verses.verse: He shall deliver you from the snare of the hunter *
verses.halfverse: and from the deadly pestilence.

verses.verse: He shall cover you with his pinions, and you shall find refuge under his wings; *
verses.halfverse: his faithfulness shall be a shield and buckler.

verses.verse: You shall not be afraid of any terror by night, *
verses.halfverse: nor of the arrow that flies by day;

verses.verse: Of the plague that stalks in the darkness, *
verses.halfverse: nor of the sickness that lays waste at mid-day.

verses.verse: A thousand shall fall at your side and ten thousand at your right hand, *
verses.halfverse: but it shall not come near you.

verses.verse: Your eyes have only to behold *
verses.halfverse: to see the reward of the wicked.

verses.verse: Because you have made the LORD your refuge, *
verses.halfverse: and the Most High your habitation,

verses.verse: There shall no evil happen to you, *
verses.halfverse: neither shall any plague come near your dwelling.

verses.verse: For he shall give his angels charge over you, *
verses.halfverse: to keep you in all your ways.

verses.verse: They shall bear you in their hands, *
verses.halfverse: lest you dash your foot against a stone.

verses.verse: You shall tread upon the lion and adder; *
verses.halfverse: you shall trample the young lion and the serpent under your feet.

verses.verse: Because he is bound to me in love, therefore will I deliver him; *
verses.halfverse: I will protect him, because he knows my Name.

verses.verse: He shall call upon me, and I will answer him; *
verses.halfverse: I am with him in trouble; I will rescue him and bring him to honor.

verses.verse: With long life will I satisfy him, *
verses.halfverse: and show him my salvation.

verses.verse: It is a good thing to give thanks to the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and to sing praises to your Name, O Most High;

verses.verse: To tell of your loving-kindness early in the morning *
verses.halfverse: and of your faithfulness in the night season;

verses.verse: On the psaltery, and on the lyre, *
verses.halfverse: and to the melody of the harp.

verses.verse: For you have made me glad by your acts, O LORD; *
verses.halfverse: and I shout for joy because of the works of your hands.

verses.verse: LORD, how great are your works! *
verses.halfverse: your thoughts are very deep.

verses.verse: The dullard does not know, nor does the fool understand, *
verses.halfverse: that though the wicked grow like weeds, and all the workers of iniquity flourish,

verses.verse: They flourish only to be destroyed for ever; *
verses.halfverse: but you, O LORD, are exalted for evermore.

verses.verse: For lo, your enemies, O LORD, lo, your enemies shall perish, *
verses.halfverse: and all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.

verses.verse: But my horn you have exalted like the horns of wild bulls; *
verses.halfverse: I am anointed with fresh oil.

verses.verse: My eyes also gloat over my enemies, *
verses.halfverse: and my ears rejoice to hear the doom of the wicked who rise up against me.

verses.verse: The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree, *
verses.halfverse: and shall spread abroad like a cedar of Lebanon.

verses.verse: Those who are planted in the house of the LORD *
verses.halfverse: shall flourish in the courts of our God;

verses.verse: They shall still bear fruit in old age; *
verses.halfverse: they shall be green and succulent;

verses.verse: That they may show how upright the LORD is, *
verses.halfverse: my Rock, in whom there is no fault.

verses.verse: The LORD is King; he has put on splendid apparel; *
verses.halfverse: the LORD has put on his apparel and girded himself with strength.

verses.verse: He has made the whole world so sure *
verses.halfverse: that it cannot be moved;

verses.verse: Ever since the world began, your throne has been established; *
verses.halfverse: you are from everlasting.

verses.verse: The waters have lifted up, O LORD, the waters have lifted up their voice; *
verses.halfverse: the waters have lifted up their pounding waves.

verses.verse: Mightier than the sound of many waters, mightier than the breakers of the sea, *
verses.halfverse: mightier is the LORD who dwells on high.

verses.verse: Your testimonies are very sure, *
verses.halfverse: and holiness adorns your house, O LORD, for ever and for evermore.

verses.verse: O LORD God of vengeance, *
verses.halfverse: O God of vengeance, show yourself.

verses.verse: Rise up, O Judge of the world; *
verses.halfverse: give the arrogant their just deserts.

verses.verse: How long shall the wicked, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: how long shall the wicked triumph?

verses.verse: They bluster in their insolence; *
verses.halfverse: all evildoers are full of boasting.

verses.verse: They crush your people, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and afflict your chosen nation.

verses.verse: They murder the widow and the stranger *
verses.halfverse: and put the orphans to death.

verses.verse: Yet they say, "The LORD does not see, *
verses.halfverse: the God of Jacob takes no notice."

verses.verse: Consider well, you dullards among the people; *
verses.halfverse: when will you fools understand?

verses.verse: He that planted the ear, does he not hear? *
verses.halfverse: he that formed the eye, does he not see?

verses.verse: He who admonishes the nations, will he not punish? *
verses.halfverse: he who teaches all the world, has he no knowledge?

verses.verse: The LORD knows our human thoughts; *
verses.halfverse: how like a puff of wind they are.

verses.verse: Happy are they whom you instruct, O Lord! *
verses.halfverse: whom you teach out of your law;

verses.verse: To give them rest in evil days, *
verses.halfverse: until a pit is dug for the wicked.

verses.verse: For the LORD will not abandon his people, *
verses.halfverse: nor will he forsake his own.

verses.verse: For judgment will again be just, *
verses.halfverse: and all the true of heart will follow it.

verses.verse: Who rose up for me against the wicked? *
verses.halfverse: who took my part against the evildoers?

verses.verse: If the LORD had not come to my help, *
verses.halfverse: I should soon have dwelt in the land of silence.

verses.verse: As often as I said, "My foot has slipped," *
verses.halfverse: your love, O LORD, upheld me.

verses.verse: When many cares fill my mind, *
verses.halfverse: your consolations cheer my soul.

verses.verse: Can a corrupt tribunal have any part with you, *
verses.halfverse: one which frames evil into law?

verses.verse: They conspire against the life of the just *
verses.halfverse: and condemn the innocent to death.

verses.verse: But the LORD has become my stronghold, *
verses.halfverse: and my God the rock of my trust.

verses.verse: He will turn their wickedness back upon them and destroy them in their own malice; *
verses.halfverse: the LORD our God will destroy them.

verses.verse: Come, let us sing to the LORD; *
verses.halfverse: let us shout for joy to the Rock of our salvation.

verses.verse: Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving *
verses.halfverse: and raise a loud shout to him with psalms.

verses.verse: For the LORD is a great God, *
verses.halfverse: and a great King above all gods.

verses.verse: In his hand are the caverns of the earth, *
verses.halfverse: and the heights of the hills are his also.

verses.verse: The sea is his, for he made it, *
verses.halfverse: and his hands have molded the dry land.

verses.verse: Come, let us bow down, and bend the knee, *
verses.halfverse: and kneel before the LORD our Maker.

verses.verse: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand. *
verses.halfverse: Oh, that today you would hearken to his voice!

verses.verse: Harden not your hearts, as your forebears did in the wilderness, *
verses.halfverse: at Meribah, and on that day at Massah, when they tempted me.

verses.verse: They put me to the test, *
verses.halfverse: though they had seen my works.

verses.verse: Forty years long I detested that generation and said, *
verses.halfverse: "This people are wayward in their hearts; they do not know my ways."

verses.verse: So I swore in my wrath, *
verses.halfverse: "They shall not enter into my rest."

verses.verse: Sing to the LORD a new song; *
verses.halfverse: sing to the LORD, all the whole earth.

verses.verse: Sing to the LORD and bless his Name; *
verses.halfverse: proclaim the good news of his salvation from day to day.

verses.verse: Declare his glory among the nations *
verses.halfverse: and his wonders among all peoples.

verses.verse: For great is the LORD and greatly to be praised; *
verses.halfverse: he is more to be feared than all gods.

verses.verse: As for all the gods of the nations, they are but idols; *
verses.halfverse: but it is the LORD who made the heavens.

verses.verse: Oh, the majesty and magnificence of his presence! *
verses.halfverse: Oh, the power and the splendor of his sanctuary!

verses.verse: Ascribe to the LORD, you families of the peoples; *
verses.halfverse: ascribe to the LORD honor and power.

verses.verse: Ascribe to the LORD the honor due his Name; *
verses.halfverse: bring offerings and come into his courts.

verses.verse: Worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness; *
verses.halfverse: let the whole earth tremble before him.

verses.verse: Tell it out among the nations: "The LORD is King! *
verses.halfverse: he has made the world so firm that it cannot be moved; he will judge the peoples with equity."

verses.verse: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea thunder and all that is in it; *
verses.halfverse: let the field be joyful and all that is therein.

verses.verse: Then shall all the trees of the wood shout for joy before the LORD when he comes, *
verses.halfverse: when he comes to judge the earth.

verses.verse: He will judge the world with righteousness *
verses.halfverse: and the peoples with his truth.

verses.verse: The LORD is King; let the earth rejoice; *
verses.halfverse: let the multitude of the isles be glad.

verses.verse: Clouds and darkness are round about him, *
verses.halfverse: righteousness and justice are the foundations of his throne.

verses.verse: A fire goes before him *
verses.halfverse: and burns up his enemies on every side.

verses.verse: His lightnings light up the world; *
verses.halfverse: the earth sees it and is afraid.

verses.verse: The mountains melt like wax at the presence of the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

verses.verse: The heavens declare his righteousness, *
verses.halfverse: and all the peoples see his glory.

verses.verse: Confounded be all who worship carved images and delight in false gods! *
verses.halfverse: Bow down before him, all you gods.

verses.verse: Zion hears and is glad, and the cities of Judah rejoice, *
verses.halfverse: because of your judgments, O LORD.

verses.verse: For you are the LORD, most high over all the earth; *
verses.halfverse: you are exalted far above all gods.

verses.verse: The LORD loves those who hate evil; *
verses.halfverse: he preserves the lives of his saints and delivers them from the hand of the wicked.

verses.verse: Light has sprung up for the righteous, *
verses.halfverse: and joyful gladness for those who are truehearted.

verses.verse: Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous, *
verses.halfverse: and give thanks to his holy Name.

verses.verse: Sing to the LORD a new song, *
verses.halfverse: for he has done marvelous things.

verses.verse: With his right hand and his holy arm *
verses.halfverse: has he won for himself the victory.

verses.verse: The LORD has made known his victory; *
verses.halfverse: his righteousness has he openly shown in the sight of the nations.

verses.verse: He remembers his mercy and faithfulness to the house of Israel, *
verses.halfverse: and all the ends of the earth have seen the victory of our God.

verses.verse: Shout with joy to the LORD, all you lands; *
verses.halfverse: lift up your voice, rejoice, and sing.

verses.verse: Sing to the LORD with the harp, *
verses.halfverse: with the harp and the voice of song.

verses.verse: With trumpets and the sound of the horn *
verses.halfverse: shout with joy before the King, the LORD.

verses.verse: Let the sea make a noise and all that is in it, *
verses.halfverse: the lands and those who dwell therein.

verses.verse: Let the rivers clap their hands, *
verses.halfverse: and let the hills ring out with joy before the LORD, when he comes to judge the earth.

verses.verse: In righteousness shall he judge the world *
verses.halfverse: and the peoples with equity.

verses.verse: The LORD is King; let the people tremble; *
verses.halfverse: he is enthroned upon the cherubim; let the earth shake.

verses.verse: The LORD is great in Zion; *
verses.halfverse: he is high above all peoples.

verses.verse: Let them confess his Name, which is great and awesome; *
verses.halfverse: he is the Holy One.

verses.verse: "O mighty King, lover of justice, you have established equity; *
verses.halfverse: you have executed justice and righteousness in Jacob."

verses.verse: Proclaim the greatness of the LORD our God and fall down before his footstool; *
verses.halfverse: he is the Holy One.

verses.verse: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among those who call upon his Name, *
verses.halfverse: they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.

verses.verse: He spoke to them out of the pillar of cloud; *
verses.halfverse: they kept his testimonies and the decree that he gave them.

verses.verse: "O LORD our God, you answered them indeed; *
verses.halfverse: you were a God who forgave them, yet punished them for their evil deeds."

verses.verse: Proclaim the greatness of the LORD our God and worship him upon his holy hill; *
verses.halfverse: for the LORD our God is the Holy One.

verses.verse: Be joyful in the LORD, all you lands; *
verses.halfverse: serve the LORD with gladness and come before his presence with a song.

verses.verse: Know this: The LORD himself is God; *
verses.halfverse: he himself has made us, and we are his; we are his people and the sheep of his pasture.

verses.verse: Enter his gates with thanksgiving; go into his courts with praise; *
verses.halfverse: give thanks to him and call upon his Name.

verses.verse: For the LORD is good; his mercy is everlasting; *
verses.halfverse: and his faithfulness endures from age to age.

verses.verse: I will sing of mercy and justice; *
verses.halfverse: to you, O LORD, will I sing praises.

verses.verse: I will strive to follow a blameless course; oh, when will you come to me? *
verses.halfverse: I will walk with sincerity of heart within my house.

verses.verse: I will set no worthless thing before my eyes; *
verses.halfverse: I hate the doers of evil deeds; they shall not remain with me.

verses.verse: A crooked heart shall be far from me; *
verses.halfverse: I will not know evil.

verses.verse: Those who in secret slander their neighbors I will destroy; *
verses.halfverse: those who have a haughty look and a proud heart I cannot abide.

verses.verse: My eyes are upon the faithful in the land, that they may dwell with me, *
verses.halfverse: and only those who lead a blameless life shall be my servants.

verses.verse: Those who act deceitfully shall not dwell in my house, *
verses.halfverse: and those who tell lies shall not continue in my sight.

verses.verse: I will soon destroy all the wicked in the land, *
verses.halfverse: that I may root out all evildoers from the city of the LORD.

verses.verse: LORD, hear my prayer, and let my cry come before you; *
verses.halfverse: hide not your face from me in the day of my trouble.

verses.verse: Incline your ear to me; *
verses.halfverse: when I call, make haste to answer me,

verses.verse: For my days drift away like smoke, *
verses.halfverse: and my bones are hot as burning coals.

verses.verse: My heart is smitten like grass and withered, *
verses.halfverse: so that I forget to eat my bread.

verses.verse: Because of the voice of my groaning *
verses.halfverse: I am but skin and bones.

verses.verse: I have become like a vulture in the wilderness, *
verses.halfverse: like an owl among the ruins.

verses.verse: I lie awake and groan; *
verses.halfverse: I am like a sparrow, lonely on a house-top.

verses.verse: My enemies revile me all day long, *
verses.halfverse: and those who scoff at me have taken an oath against me.

verses.verse: For I have eaten ashes for bread *
verses.halfverse: and mingled my drink with weeping.

verses.verse: Because of your indignation and wrath *
verses.halfverse: you have lifted me up and thrown me away.

verses.verse: My days pass away like a shadow, *
verses.halfverse: and I wither like the grass.

verses.verse: But you, O LORD, endure for ever, *
verses.halfverse: and your Name from age to age.

verses.verse: You will arise and have compassion on Zion, for it is time to have mercy upon her; *
verses.halfverse: indeed, the appointed time has come.

verses.verse: For your servants love her very rubble, *
verses.halfverse: and are moved to pity even for her dust.

verses.verse: The nations shall fear your Name, O LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and all the kings of the earth your glory.

verses.verse: For the LORD will build up Zion, *
verses.halfverse: and his glory will appear.

verses.verse: He will look with favor on the prayer of the homeless; *
verses.halfverse: he will not despise their plea.

verses.verse: Let this be written for a future generation, *
verses.halfverse: so that a people yet unborn may praise the LORD.

verses.verse: For the LORD looked down from his holy place on high; *
verses.halfverse: from the heavens he beheld the earth;

verses.verse: That he might hear the groan of the captive *
verses.halfverse: and set free those condemned to die;

verses.verse: That they may declare in Zion the Name of the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and his praise in Jerusalem;

verses.verse: When the peoples are gathered together, *
verses.halfverse: and the kingdoms also, to serve the LORD.

verses.verse: He has brought down my strength before my time; *
verses.halfverse: he has shortened the number of my days;

verses.verse: And I said, "O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days; *
verses.halfverse: your years endure throughout all generations.

verses.verse: In the beginning, O LORD, you laid the foundations of the earth, *
verses.halfverse: and the heavens are the work of your hands;

verses.verse: They shall perish, but you will endure; they all shall wear out like a garment; *
verses.halfverse: as clothing you will change them, and they shall be changed;

verses.verse: But you are always the same, *
verses.halfverse: and your years will never end.

verses.verse: The children of your servants shall continue, *
verses.halfverse: and their offspring shall stand fast in your sight."

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, O my soul, *
verses.halfverse: and all that is within me, bless his holy Name.

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, O my soul, *
verses.halfverse: and forget not all his benefits.

verses.verse: He forgives all your sins *
verses.halfverse: and heals all your infirmities;

verses.verse: He redeems your life from the grave *
verses.halfverse: and crowns you with mercy and loving-kindness;

verses.verse: He satisfies you with good things, *
verses.halfverse: and your youth is renewed like an eagle's.

verses.verse: The LORD executes righteousness *
verses.halfverse: and judgment for all who are oppressed.

verses.verse: He made his ways known to Moses *
verses.halfverse: and his works to the children of Israel.

verses.verse: The LORD is full of compassion and mercy, *
verses.halfverse: slow to anger and of great kindness.

verses.verse: He will not always accuse us, *
verses.halfverse: nor will he keep his anger for ever.

verses.verse: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, *
verses.halfverse: nor rewarded us according to our wickedness.

verses.verse: For as the heavens are high above the earth, *
verses.halfverse: so is his mercy great upon those who fear him.

verses.verse: As far as the east is from the west, *
verses.halfverse: so far has he removed our sins from us.

verses.verse: As a father cares for his children, *
verses.halfverse: so does the LORD care for those who fear him.

verses.verse: For he himself knows whereof we are made; *
verses.halfverse: he remembers that we are but dust.

verses.verse: Our days are like the grass; *
verses.halfverse: we flourish like a flower of the field;

verses.verse: When the wind goes over it, it is gone, *
verses.halfverse: and its place shall know it no more.

verses.verse: But the merciful goodness of the LORD endures for ever on those who fear him, *
verses.halfverse: and his righteousness on children's children;

verses.verse: On those who keep his covenant *
verses.halfverse: and remember his commandments and do them.

verses.verse: The LORD has set his throne in heaven, *
verses.halfverse: and his kingship has dominion over all.

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, you angels of his, you mighty ones who do his bidding, *
verses.halfverse: and hearken to the voice of his word.

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, all you his hosts, *
verses.halfverse: you ministers of his who do his will.

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, all you works of his, in all places of his dominion; *
verses.halfverse: bless the LORD, O my soul.

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, O my soul; *
verses.halfverse: O LORD my God, how excellent is your greatness! you are clothed with majesty and splendor.

verses.verse: You wrap yourself with light as with a cloak *
verses.halfverse: and spread out the heavens like a curtain.

verses.verse: You lay the beams of your chambers in the waters above; *
verses.halfverse: you make the clouds your chariot; you ride on the wings of the wind.

verses.verse: You make the winds your messengers *
verses.halfverse: and flames of fire your servants.

verses.verse: You have set the earth upon its foundations, *
verses.halfverse: so that it never shall move at any time.

verses.verse: You covered it with the Deep as with a mantle; *
verses.halfverse: the waters stood higher than the mountains.

verses.verse: At your rebuke they fled; *
verses.halfverse: at the voice of your thunder they hastened away.

verses.verse: They went up into the hills and down to the valleys beneath, *
verses.halfverse: to the places you had appointed for them.

verses.verse: You set the limits that they should not pass; *
verses.halfverse: they shall not again cover the earth.

verses.verse: You send the springs into the valleys; *
verses.halfverse: they flow between the mountains.

verses.verse: All the beasts of the field drink their fill from them, *
verses.halfverse: and the wild asses quench their thirst.

verses.verse: Beside them the birds of the air make their nests *
verses.halfverse: and sing among the branches.

verses.verse: You water the mountains from your dwelling on high; *
verses.halfverse: the earth is fully satisfied by the fruit of your works.

verses.verse: You make grass grow for flocks and herds *
verses.halfverse: and plants to serve mankind;

verses.verse: That they may bring forth food from the earth, *
verses.halfverse: and wine to gladden our hearts,

verses.verse: Oil to make a cheerful countenance, *
verses.halfverse: and bread to strengthen the heart.

verses.verse: The trees of the LORD are full of sap, *
verses.halfverse: the cedars of Lebanon which he planted,

verses.verse: In which the birds build their nests, *
verses.halfverse: and in whose tops the stork makes his dwelling.

verses.verse: The high hills are a refuge for the mountain goats, *
verses.halfverse: and the stony cliffs for the rock badgers.

verses.verse: You appointed the moon to mark the seasons, *
verses.halfverse: and the sun knows the time of its setting.

verses.verse: You make darkness that it may be night, *
verses.halfverse: in which all the beasts of the forest prowl.

verses.verse: The lions roar after their prey *
verses.halfverse: and seek their food from God.

verses.verse: The sun rises, and they slip away *
verses.halfverse: and lay themselves down in their dens.

verses.verse: Man goes forth to his work *
verses.halfverse: and to his labor until the evening.

verses.verse: O LORD, how manifold are your works! *
verses.halfverse: in wisdom you have made them all; the earth is full of your creatures.

verses.verse: Yonder is the great and wide sea with its living things too many to number, *
verses.halfverse: creatures both small and great.

verses.verse: There move the ships, and there is that Leviathan, *
verses.halfverse: which you have made for the sport of it.

verses.verse: All of them look to you *
verses.halfverse: to give them their food in due season.

verses.verse: You give it to them; they gather it; *
verses.halfverse: you open your hand, and they are filled with good things.

verses.verse: You hide your face, and they are terrified; *
verses.halfverse: you take away their breath, and they die and return to their dust.

verses.verse: You send forth your Spirit, and they are created; *
verses.halfverse: and so you renew the face of the earth.

verses.verse: May the glory of the LORD endure for ever; *
verses.halfverse: may the LORD rejoice in all his works.

verses.verse: He looks at the earth and it trembles; *
verses.halfverse: he touches the mountains and they smoke.

verses.verse: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; *
verses.halfverse: I will praise my God while I have my being.

verses.verse: May these words of mine please him; *
verses.halfverse: I will rejoice in the LORD.

verses.verse: Let sinners be consumed out of the earth, *
verses.halfverse: and the wicked be no more.

verses.verse: Bless the LORD, O my soul. *
verses.halfverse: Hallelujah!

verses.verse: Give thanks to the LORD and call upon his Name; *
verses.halfverse: make known his deeds among the peoples.

verses.verse: Sing to him, sing praises to him, *
verses.halfverse: and speak of all his marvelous works.

verses.verse: Glory in his holy Name; *
verses.halfverse: let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice.

verses.verse: Search for the LORD and his strength; *
verses.halfverse: continually seek his face.

verses.verse: Remember the marvels he has done, *
verses.halfverse: his wonders and the judgments of his mouth,

verses.verse: O offspring of Abraham his servant, *
verses.halfverse: O children of Jacob his chosen.

verses.verse: He is the LORD our God; *
verses.halfverse: his judgments prevail in all the world.

verses.verse: He has always been mindful of his covenant, *
verses.halfverse: the promise he made for a thousand generations:

verses.verse: The covenant he made with Abraham, *
verses.halfverse: the oath that he swore to Isaac,

verses.verse: Which he established as a statute for Jacob, *
verses.halfverse: an everlasting covenant for Israel,

verses.verse: Saying, "To you will I give the land of Canaan *
verses.halfverse: to be your allotted inheritance."

verses.verse: When they were few in number, *
verses.halfverse: of little account, and sojourners in the land,

verses.verse: Wandering from nation to nation *
verses.halfverse: and from one kingdom to another,

verses.verse: He let no one oppress them *
verses.halfverse: and rebuked kings for their sake,

verses.verse: Saying, "Do not touch my anointed *
verses.halfverse: and do my prophets no harm."

verses.verse: Then he called for a famine in the land *
verses.halfverse: and destroyed the supply of bread.

verses.verse: He sent a man before them, *
verses.halfverse: Joseph, who was sold as a slave.

verses.verse: They bruised his feet in fetters; *
verses.halfverse: his neck they put in an iron collar.

verses.verse: Until his prediction came to pass, *
verses.halfverse: the word of the LORD tested him.

verses.verse: The king sent and released him; *
verses.halfverse: the ruler of the peoples set him free.

verses.verse: He set him as a master over his household, *
verses.halfverse: as a ruler over all his possessions,

verses.verse: To instruct his princes according to his will *
verses.halfverse: and to teach his elders wisdom.

verses.verse: Israel came into Egypt, *
verses.halfverse: and Jacob became a sojourner in the land of Ham.

verses.verse: The LORD made his people exceedingly fruitful; *
verses.halfverse: he made them stronger than their enemies;

verses.verse: Whose heart he turned, so that they hated his people, *
verses.halfverse: and dealt unjustly with his servants.

verses.verse: He sent Moses his servant, *
verses.halfverse: and Aaron whom he had chosen.

verses.verse: They worked his signs among them, *
verses.halfverse: and portents in the land of Ham.

verses.verse: He sent darkness, and it grew dark; *
verses.halfverse: but the Egyptians rebelled against his words.

verses.verse: He turned their waters into blood *
verses.halfverse: and caused their fish to die.

verses.verse: Their land was overrun by frogs, *
verses.halfverse: in the very chambers of their kings.

verses.verse: He spoke, and there came swarms of insects *
verses.halfverse: and gnats within all their borders.

verses.verse: He gave them hailstones instead of rain, *
verses.halfverse: and flames of fire throughout their land.

verses.verse: He blasted their vines and their fig trees *
verses.halfverse: and shattered every tree in their country.

verses.verse: He spoke, and the locust came, *
verses.halfverse: and young locusts without number,

verses.verse: Which ate up all the green plants in their land *
verses.halfverse: and devoured the fruit of their soil.

verses.verse: He struck down the firstborn of their land, *
verses.halfverse: the firstfruits of all their strength.

verses.verse: He led out his people with silver and gold; *
verses.halfverse: in all their tribes there was not one that stumbled.

verses.verse: Egypt was glad of their going, *
verses.halfverse: because they were afraid of them.

verses.verse: He spread out a cloud for a covering *
verses.halfverse: and a fire to give light in the night season.

verses.verse: They asked, and quails appeared, *
verses.halfverse: and he satisfied them with bread from heaven.

verses.verse: He opened the rock, and water flowed, *
verses.halfverse: so the river ran in the dry places.

verses.verse: For God remembered his holy word *
verses.halfverse: and Abraham his servant.

verses.verse: So he led forth his people with gladness, *
verses.halfverse: his chosen with shouts of joy.

verses.verse: He gave his people the lands of the nations, *
verses.halfverse: and they took the fruit of others' toil,

verses.verse: That they might keep his statutes *
verses.halfverse: and observe his laws. Hallelujah!

verses.verse: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, *
verses.halfverse: for his mercy endures for ever.

verses.verse: Who can declare the mighty acts of the LORD *
verses.halfverse: or show forth all his praise?

verses.verse: Happy are those who act with justice *
verses.halfverse: and always do what is right!

verses.verse: Remember me, O LORD, with the favor you have for your people, *
verses.halfverse: and visit me with your saving help;

verses.verse: That I may see the prosperity of your elect and be glad with the gladness of your people, *
verses.halfverse: that I may glory with your inheritance.

verses.verse: We have sinned as our forebears did; *
verses.halfverse: we have done wrong and dealt wickedly.

verses.verse: In Egypt they did not consider your marvelous works, nor remember the abundance of your love; *
verses.halfverse: they defied the Most High at the Red Sea.

verses.verse: But he saved them for his Name's sake, *
verses.halfverse: to make his power known.

verses.verse: He rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up, *
verses.halfverse: and he led them through the deep as through a desert.

verses.verse: He saved them from the hand of those who hated them *
verses.halfverse: and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.

verses.verse: The waters covered their oppressors; *
verses.halfverse: not one of them was left.

verses.verse: Then they believed his words *
verses.halfverse: and sang him songs of praise.

verses.verse: But they soon forgot his deeds *
verses.halfverse: and did not wait for his counsel.

verses.verse: A craving seized them in the wilderness, *
verses.halfverse: and they put God to the test in the desert.

verses.verse: He gave them what they asked, *
verses.halfverse: but sent leanness into their soul.

verses.verse: They envied Moses in the camp, *
verses.halfverse: and Aaron, the holy one of the LORD.

verses.verse: The earth opened and swallowed Dathan *
verses.halfverse: and covered the company of Abiram.

verses.verse: Fire blazed up against their company, *
verses.halfverse: and flames devoured the wicked.

verses.verse: Israel made a bull-calf at Horeb *
verses.halfverse: and worshiped a molten image;

verses.verse: And so they exchanged their Glory *
verses.halfverse: for the image of an ox that feeds on grass.

verses.verse: They forgot God their Savior, *
verses.halfverse: who had done great things in Egypt,

verses.verse: Wonderful deeds in the land of Ham, *
verses.halfverse: and fearful things at the Red Sea.

verses.verse: So he would have destroyed them, had not Moses his chosen stood before him in the breach, *
verses.halfverse: to turn away his wrath from consuming them.

verses.verse: They refused the pleasant land *
verses.halfverse: and would not believe his promise.

verses.verse: They grumbled in their tents *
verses.halfverse: and would not listen to the voice of the LORD.

verses.verse: So he lifted his hand against them, *
verses.halfverse: to overthrow them in the wilderness,

verses.verse: To cast out their seed among the nations, *
verses.halfverse: and to scatter them throughout the lands.

verses.verse: They joined themselves to Baal-Peor *
verses.halfverse: and ate sacrifices offered to the dead.

verses.verse: They provoked him to anger with their actions, *
verses.halfverse: and a plague broke out among them.

verses.verse: Then Phinehas stood up and interceded, *
verses.halfverse: and the plague came to an end.

verses.verse: This was reckoned to him as righteousness *
verses.halfverse: throughout all generations for ever.

verses.verse: Again they provoked his anger at the waters of Meribah, *
verses.halfverse: so that he punished Moses because of them;

verses.verse: For they so embittered his spirit *
verses.halfverse: that he spoke rash words with his lips.

verses.verse: They did not destroy the peoples *
verses.halfverse: as the LORD had commanded them.

verses.verse: They intermingled with the heathen *
verses.halfverse: and learned their pagan ways,

verses.verse: So that they worshiped their idols, *
verses.halfverse: which became a snare to them.

verses.verse: They sacrificed their sons *
verses.halfverse: and their daughters to evil spirits.

verses.verse: They shed innocent blood, the blood of their sons and daughters, *
verses.halfverse: which they offered to the idols of Canaan, and the land was defiled with blood.

verses.verse: Thus they were polluted by their actions *
verses.halfverse: and went whoring in their evil deeds.

verses.verse: Therefore the wrath of the LORD was kindled against his people *
verses.halfverse: and he abhorred his inheritance.

verses.verse: He gave them over to the hand of the heathen, *
verses.halfverse: and those who hated them ruled over them.

verses.verse: Their enemies oppressed them, *
verses.halfverse: and they were humbled under their hand.

verses.verse: Many a time did he deliver them, but they rebelled through their own devices, *
verses.halfverse: and were brought down in their iniquity.

verses.verse: Nevertheless, he saw their distress, *
verses.halfverse: when he heard their lamentation.

verses.verse: He remembered his covenant with them *
verses.halfverse: and relented in accordance with his great mercy.

verses.verse: He caused them to be pitied *
verses.halfverse: by those who held them captive.

verses.verse: Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the nations, *
verses.halfverse: that we may give thanks to your holy Name and glory in your praise.

verses.verse: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting; *
verses.halfverse: and let all the people say, "Amen!" Hallelujah!

verses.verse: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, *
verses.halfverse: and his mercy endures for ever.

verses.verse: Let all those whom the LORD has redeemed proclaim *
verses.halfverse: that he redeemed them from the hand of the foe.

verses.verse: He gathered them out of the lands; *
verses.halfverse: from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.

verses.verse: Some wandered in desert wastes; *
verses.halfverse: they found no way to a city where they might dwell.

verses.verse: They were hungry and thirsty; *
verses.halfverse: their spirits languished within them.

verses.verse: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, *
verses.halfverse: and he delivered them from their distress.

verses.verse: He put their feet on a straight path *
verses.halfverse: to go to a city where they might dwell.

verses.verse: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his mercy *
verses.halfverse: and the wonders he does for his children.

verses.verse: For he satisfies the thirsty *
verses.halfverse: and fills the hungry with good things.

verses.verse: Some sat in darkness and deep gloom, *
verses.halfverse: bound fast in misery and iron;

verses.verse: Because they rebelled against the words of God *
verses.halfverse: and despised the counsel of the Most High.

verses.verse: So he humbled their spirits with hard labor; *
verses.halfverse: they stumbled, and there was none to help.

verses.verse: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, *
verses.halfverse: and he delivered them from their distress.

verses.verse: He led them out of darkness and deep gloom *
verses.halfverse: and broke their bonds asunder.

verses.verse: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his mercy *
verses.halfverse: and the wonders he does for his children.

verses.verse: For he shatters the doors of bronze *
verses.halfverse: and breaks in two the iron bars.

verses.verse: Some were fools and took to rebellious ways; *
verses.halfverse: they were afflicted because of their sins.

verses.verse: They abhorred all manner of food *
verses.halfverse: and drew near to death's door.

verses.verse: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, *
verses.halfverse: and he delivered them from their distress.

verses.verse: He sent forth his word and healed them *
verses.halfverse: and saved them from the grave.

verses.verse: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his mercy *
verses.halfverse: and the wonders he does for his children.

verses.verse: Let them offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving *
verses.halfverse: and tell of his acts with shouts of joy.

verses.verse: Some went down to the sea in ships *
verses.halfverse: and plied their trade in deep waters;

verses.verse: They beheld the works of the LORD *
verses.halfverse: and his wonders in the deep.

verses.verse: Then he spoke, and a stormy wind arose, *
verses.halfverse: which tossed high the waves of the sea.

verses.verse: They mounted up to the heavens and fell back to the depths; *
verses.halfverse: their hearts melted because of their peril.

verses.verse: They reeled and staggered like drunkards *
verses.halfverse: and were at their wits' end.

verses.verse: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, *
verses.halfverse: and he delivered them from their distress.

verses.verse: He stilled the storm to a whisper *
verses.halfverse: and quieted the waves of the sea.

verses.verse: Then were they glad because of the calm, *
verses.halfverse: and he brought them to the harbor they were bound for.

verses.verse: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his mercy *
verses.halfverse: and the wonders he does for his children.

verses.verse: Let them exalt him in the congregation of the people *
verses.halfverse: and praise him in the council of the elders.

verses.verse: The LORD changed rivers into deserts, *
verses.halfverse: and water-springs into thirsty ground,

verses.verse: A fruitful land into salt flats, *
verses.halfverse: because of the wickedness of those who dwell there.

verses.verse: He changed deserts into pools of water *
verses.halfverse: and dry land into water-springs.

verses.verse: He settled the hungry there, *
verses.halfverse: and they founded a city to dwell in.

verses.verse: They sowed fields, and planted vineyards, *
verses.halfverse: and brought in a fruitful harvest.

verses.verse: He blessed them, so that they increased greatly; *
verses.halfverse: he did not let their herds decrease.

verses.verse: Yet when they were diminished and brought low, *
verses.halfverse: through stress of adversity and sorrow,

verses.verse: (He pours contempt on princes *
verses.halfverse: and makes them wander in trackless wastes)

verses.verse: He lifted up the poor out of misery *
verses.halfverse: and multiplied their families like flocks of sheep.

verses.verse: The upright will see this and rejoice, *
verses.halfverse: but all wickedness will shut its mouth.

verses.verse: Whoever is wise will ponder these things, *
verses.halfverse: and consider well the mercies of the LORD.

verses.verse: My heart is firmly fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; *
verses.halfverse: I will sing and make melody.

verses.verse: Wake up, my spirit; awake, lute and harp; *
verses.halfverse: I myself will waken the dawn.

verses.verse: I will confess you among the peoples, O LORD; *
verses.halfverse: I will sing praises to you among the nations.

verses.verse: For your loving-kindness is greater than the heavens, *
verses.halfverse: and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.

verses.verse: Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, *
verses.halfverse: and your glory over all the earth.

verses.verse: So that those who are dear to you may be delivered, *
verses.halfverse: save with your right hand and answer me.

verses.verse: God spoke from his holy place and said, *
verses.halfverse: "I will exult and parcel out Shechem; I will divide the valley of Succoth.

verses.verse: Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; *
verses.halfverse: Ephraim is my helmet and Judah my scepter.

verses.verse: Moab is my washbasin, on Edom I throw down my sandal to claim it, *
verses.halfverse: and over Philistia will I shout in triumph."

verses.verse: Who will lead me into the strong city? *
verses.halfverse: who will bring me into Edom?

verses.verse: Have you not cast us off, O God? *
verses.halfverse: you no longer go out, O God, with our armies.

verses.verse: Grant us your help against the enemy, *
verses.halfverse: for vain is the help of man.

verses.verse: With God we will do valiant deeds, *
verses.halfverse: and he shall tread our enemies under foot.

verses.verse: Hold not your tongue, O God of my praise; *
verses.halfverse: for the mouth of the wicked, the mouth of the deceitful, is opened against me.

verses.verse: They speak to me with a lying tongue; *
verses.halfverse: they encompass me with hateful words and fight against me without a cause.

verses.verse: Despite my love, they accuse me; *
verses.halfverse: but as for me, I pray for them.

verses.verse: They repay evil for good, *
verses.halfverse: and hatred for my love.

verses.verse: Set a wicked man against him, *
verses.halfverse: and let an accuser stand at his right hand.

verses.verse: When he is judged, let him be found guilty, *
verses.halfverse: and let his appeal be in vain.

verses.verse: Let his days be few, *
verses.halfverse: and let another take his office.

verses.verse: Let his children be fatherless, *
verses.halfverse: and his wife become a widow.

verses.verse: Let his children be waifs and beggars; *
verses.halfverse: let them be driven from the ruins of their homes.

verses.verse: Let the creditor seize everything he has; *
verses.halfverse: let strangers plunder his gains.

verses.verse: Let there be no one to show him kindness, *
verses.halfverse: and none to pity his fatherless children.

verses.verse: Let his descendants be destroyed, *
verses.halfverse: and his name be blotted out in the next generation.

verses.verse: Let the wickedness of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: and his mother's sin not be blotted out;

verses.verse: Let their sin be always before the LORD; *
verses.halfverse: but let him root out their names from the earth;

verses.verse: Because he did not remember to show mercy, *
verses.halfverse: but persecuted the poor and needy and sought to kill the brokenhearted.

verses.verse: He loved cursing, let it come upon him; *
verses.halfverse: he took no delight in blessing, let it depart from him.

verses.verse: He put on cursing like a garment, *
verses.halfverse: let it soak into his body like water and into his bones like oil;

verses.verse: Let it be to him like the cloak which he wraps around himself, *
verses.halfverse: and like the belt that he wears continually.

verses.verse: Let this be the recompense from the LORD to my accusers, *
verses.halfverse: and to those who speak evil against me.

verses.verse: But you, O Lord my God, oh, deal with me according to your Name; *
verses.halfverse: for your tender mercy's sake, deliver me.

verses.verse: For I am poor and needy, *
verses.halfverse: and my heart is wounded within me.

verses.verse: I have faded away like a shadow when it lengthens; *
verses.halfverse: I am shaken off like a locust.

verses.verse: My knees are weak through fasting, *
verses.halfverse: and my flesh is wasted and gaunt.

verses.verse: I have become a reproach to them; *
verses.halfverse: they see and shake their heads.

verses.verse: Help me, O LORD my God; *
verses.halfverse: save me for your mercy's sake.

verses.verse: Let them know that this is your hand, *
verses.halfverse: that you, O LORD, have done it.

verses.verse: They may curse, but you will bless; *
verses.halfverse: let those who rise up against me be put to shame, and your servant will rejoice.

verses.verse: Let my accusers be clothed with disgrace *
verses.halfverse: and wrap themselves in their shame as in a cloak.

verses.verse: I will give great thanks to the LORD with my mouth; *
verses.halfverse: in the midst of the multitude will I praise him;

verses.verse: Because he stands at the right hand of the needy, *
verses.halfverse: to save his life from those who would condemn him.

verses.verse: The LORD said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand, *
verses.halfverse: until I make your enemies your footstool."

verses.verse: The LORD will send the scepter of your power out of Zion, *
verses.halfverse: saying, "Rule over your enemies round about you.

verses.verse: Princely state has been yours from the day of your birth; *
verses.halfverse: in the beauty of holiness have I begotten you, like dew from the womb of the morning."

verses.verse: The LORD has sworn and he will not recant: *
verses.halfverse: "You are a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek."

verses.verse: The Lord who is at your right hand will smite kings in the day of his wrath; *
verses.halfverse: he will rule over the nations.

verses.verse: He will heap high the corpses; *
verses.halfverse: he will smash heads over the wide earth.

verses.verse: He will drink from the brook beside the road; *
verses.halfverse: therefore he will lift high his head.

verses.verse: Hallelujah! I will give thanks to the LORD with my whole heart, *
verses.halfverse: in the assembly of the upright, in the congregation.

verses.verse: Great are the deeds of the LORD! *
verses.halfverse: they are studied by all who delight in them.

verses.verse: His work is full of majesty and splendor, *
verses.halfverse: and his righteousness endures for ever.

verses.verse: He makes his marvelous works to be remembered; *
verses.halfverse: the LORD is gracious and full of compassion.

verses.verse: He gives food to those who fear him; *
verses.halfverse: he is ever mindful of his covenant.

verses.verse: He has shown his people the power of his works *
verses.halfverse: in giving them the lands of the nations.

verses.verse: The works of his hands are faithfulness and justice; *
verses.halfverse: all his commandments are sure.

verses.verse: They stand fast for ever and ever, *
verses.halfverse: because they are done in truth and equity.

verses.verse: He sent redemption to his people; he commanded his covenant for ever; *
verses.halfverse: holy and awesome is his Name.

verses.verse: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; *
verses.halfverse: those who act accordingly have a good understanding; his praise endures for ever.

verses.verse: Hallelujah! Happy are they who fear the Lord *
verses.halfverse: and have great delight in his commandments!

verses.verse: Their descendants will be mighty in the land; *
verses.halfverse: the generation of the upright will be blessed.

verses.verse: Wealth and riches will be in their house, *
verses.halfverse: and their righteousness will last for ever.

verses.verse: Light shines in the darkness for the upright; *
verses.halfverse: the righteous are merciful and full of compassion.

verses.verse: It is good for them to be generous in lending *
verses.halfverse: and to manage their affairs with justice.

verses.verse: For they will never be shaken; *
verses.halfverse: the righteous will be kept in everlasting remembrance.

verses.verse: They will not be afraid of any evil rumors; *
verses.halfverse: their heart is right; they put their trust in the Lord.

verses.verse: Their heart is established and will not shrink, *
verses.halfverse: until they see their desire upon their enemies.

verses.verse: They have given freely to the poor, *
verses.halfverse: and their righteousness stands fast for ever; they will hold up their head with honor.

verses.verse: The wicked will see it and be angry; they will gnash their teeth and pine away; *
verses.halfverse: the desires of the wicked will perish.

verses.verse: Hallelujah! Give praise, you servants of the LORD; *
verses.halfverse: praise the Name of the LORD.

verses.verse: Let the Name of the LORD be blessed, *
verses.halfverse: from this time forth for evermore.

verses.verse: From the rising of the sun to its going down *
verses.halfverse: let the Name of the LORD be praised.

verses.verse: The LORD is high above all nations, *
verses.halfverse: and his glory above the heavens.

verses.verse: Who is like the LORD our God, who sits enthroned on high, *
verses.halfverse: but stoops to behold the heavens and the earth?

verses.verse: He takes up the weak out of the dust *
verses.halfverse: and lifts up the poor from the ashes.

verses.verse: He sets them with the princes, *
verses.halfverse: with the princes of his people.

verses.verse: He makes the woman of a childless house *
verses.halfverse: to be a joyful mother of children.

verses.verse: Hallelujah! When Israel came out of Egypt, *
verses.halfverse: the house of Jacob from a people of strange speech,

verses.verse: Judah became God's sanctuary *
verses.halfverse: and Israel his dominion.

verses.verse: The sea beheld it and fled; *
verses.halfverse: Jordan turned and went back.

verses.verse: The mountains skipped like rams, *
verses.halfverse: and the little hills like young sheep.

verses.verse: What ailed you, O sea, that you fled? *
verses.halfverse: O Jordan, that you turned back?

verses.verse: You mountains, that you skipped like rams? *
verses.halfverse: you little hills like young sheep?

verses.verse: Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, *
verses.halfverse: at the presence of the God of Jacob,

verses.verse: Who turned the hard rock into a pool of water *
verses.halfverse: and flint-stone into a flowing spring.

verses.verse: Not to us, O LORD, not to us, but to your Name give glory; *
verses.halfverse: because of your love and because of your faithfulness.

verses.verse: Why should the heathen say, *
verses.halfverse: "Where then is their God?"

verses.verse: Our God is in heaven; *
verses.halfverse: whatever he wills to do he does.

verses.verse: Their idols are silver and gold, *
verses.halfverse: the work of human hands.

verses.verse: They have mouths, but they cannot speak; *
verses.halfverse: eyes have they, but they cannot see;

verses.verse: They have ears but they cannot hear; *
verses.halfverse: noses, but they cannot smell;

verses.verse: They have hands, but they cannot feel; feet, but they cannot walk; *
verses.halfverse: they make no sound with their throat.

verses.verse: Those who make them are like them, *
verses.halfverse: and so are all who put their trust in them.

verses.verse: O Israel, trust in the LORD; *
verses.halfverse: he is their help and their shield.

verses.verse: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD; *
verses.halfverse: he is their help and their shield.

verses.verse: You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD; *
verses.halfverse: he is their help and their shield.

verses.verse: The LORD has been mindful of us, and he will bless us; *
verses.halfverse: he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;

verses.verse: He will bless those who fear the LORD, *
verses.halfverse: both small and great together.

verses.verse: May the LORD increase you more and more, *
verses.halfverse: you and your children after you.
